taux anormal de glucose-6-phosphate désh: Abnormal glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in dried blood spot
augmentation du taux de glucose-6-phosph: Increased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in dried blood spot
diminution du taux de glucose-6-phosphat: Decreased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in dried blood spot
taux anormal de glucose-6-phosphate désh: Abnormal glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in leukocytes
Abnormal glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in red blood cells
Abnormal glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in tissue
augmentation du taux de glucose-6-phosph: Increased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in tissue
diminution du taux de glucose-6-phosphat: Decreased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in tissue
Decreased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in red blood cells
augmentation du taux de glucose-6-phosph: Increased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in red blood cells
diminution du taux de glucose-6-phosphat: Decreased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in leukocytes
augmentation du taux de glucose-6-phosph: Increased glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase level in leukocytes
activité anormale de l'uridine diphospha: Abnormal uridine diphosphate glucose-4-epimerase activity in plasma
augmentation de l'activité uridine dipho: Increased uridine diphosphate glucose-4-epimerase activity in plasma
diminution de l'activité uridine diphosp: Decreased uridine diphosphate glucose-4-epimerase activity in plasma
activité anormale de l'uridine diphospha: Abnormal uridine diphosphate glucose-4-epimerase activity in red blood cells
augmentation de l'activité de l'uridine: Increased uridine diphosphate glucose-4-epimerase activity in red blood cells
diminution de l'activité uridine diphosp: Decreased uridine diphosphate glucose-4-epimerase activity in red blood cells
niveau anormal de gaz sanguin dans le sa: Abnormal blood gas level in cord blood
hypoplasie du maxillaire par rapport à l: Hypoplasia of maxilla relative to mandible
hypoplasie de la mandibule par rapport a: Hypoplasia of mandible relative to maxilla
augmentation du taux d'anticorps ige ant: Increased anti-dairy protein IgE antibody level
Increased anti-animal protein IgE antibody level
Increased anti-dust mite IgE antibody level
Increased anti-food allergen IgE antibody level
Increased anti-plant based food allergen IgE antibody level
Increased anti-nut food product IgE antibody level
Increased anti-meat allergen IgE antibody level
Increased anti-plant product IgE antibody level
Increased anti-animal dander IgE antibody level
Increased anti-house dust mite IgE antibody level
neutropénie chronique avec arrêt de matu: Chronic neutropenia in myeloid maturation arrest in bone marrow
neutropénie cyclique en cas d'arrêt de l: Cyclic neutropenia in myeloid maturation arrest in bone marrow
diminution de la réponse des anticorps s: Decreased specific antibody response to protein vaccine
absence complète ou quasi-complète de ré: Complete or near-complete absence of specific antibody response to tetanus vaccine
Complete or near-complete absence of specific antibody response to hepatitis B vaccine
absence partielle de réponse des anticor: Partial absence of specific antibody response to tetanus vaccine
Partial absence of specific antibody response to hepatitis B vaccine
diminution de la réponse en anticorps sp: Decreased specific antibody response to polysaccharide vaccine
absence totale ou quasi totale de répons: Complete or near-complete absence of specific antibody response to pneumococcus vaccine
absence partielle de réponse en anticorp: Partial absence of specific antibody response to pneumococcus vaccine
diminution de la réponse des anticorps s: Decreased specific antibody response to protein-conjugated polysaccharide vaccine
absence complète ou quasi-complète de ré: Complete or near-complete absence of specific antibody response to Haemophilus H type B vaccine
Complete or near-complete absence of specific antibody response to meningococcus vaccine
absence partielle de réponse spécifique: Partial absence of specific antibody response to Haemophilus H type B vaccine
absence partielle de réponse des anticor: Partial absence of specific antibody response to meningococcus vaccine
diminution de la réponse des anticorps s: Decreased specific antibody response to infection
anomalie du voile du palais: Abnormality of musculature of soft palate
anomalie du muscle tenseur du voile du p: Abnormality of tensor veli palatini muscle
anomalie des muscles de l'expression fac: Abnormality of muscle of facial expression
anomalie de l'élévateur de la lèvre supé: Abnormality of levator labii superioris alaeque nasi muscle
anomalie de la forme du mileu de la face: Abnormality of the shape of the midface
dépense énergétique au repos anormale d': Abnormal resting energy expenditure from metabolic cart test
diminution du taux d'acide gamma-aminobu: Reduced brain gamma-aminobutyric acid level by MRS
aplasie de la musculature du pelvis: Aplasia of the musculature of the pelvis
hypoplasie de la musculature du pelvis: Hypoplasia of the musculature of the pelvis
augmentation de la concentration plasmat: Increased level of carnosine in blood
diminution de la concentration plasmatiq: Decreased level of carnosine in blood
concentration urinaire en acylcarnithine: Abnormal concentration of acylcarnitine in the urine
anomalie du cartilage de l'oreille exter: Abnormality of cartilage of external ear
anomalie du muscle dépresseur de l'angul: Abnormality of depressor anguli oris muscle
anomalie de l'orbite osseuse du crâne: Abnormality of bony orbit of skull
anomalie du cartilage de la cloison nasa: Abnormality of cartilage of nasal septum
anomalie du processus frontal du maxilla: Abnormality of frontal process of maxilla
anomalie de l'artère de la lèvre inférie: Abnormality of artery of lower lip
anomalie du muscle oblique inférieur ext: Abnormality of inferior oblique extraocular muscle
anomalie du muscle droit inférieur extra: Abnormality of inferior rectus extraocular muscle
anomalie du muscle intrinsèque de la lan: Abnormality of intrinsic muscle of tongue
anomalie du muscle droit externe extra-o: Abnormality of lateral rectus extra-ocular muscle
anomalie du muscle releveur veli palatin: Abnormality of levator veli palatini muscle
hypospadias avec localisation du méat ur: Hypospadias with location of the urethral meatus in the perineal region
anomalie congénitale caractérisée par la: Congenital anomaly characterized by closure or failure to develop an opening in the urethra in males
pénis anormalement petit. à la naissance: Abnormally small penis. At birth, the normal penis is about 3 cm (stretched length from pubic tubercle to tip of penis) with micropenis less than 2.0-2.5 cm
anomalie des lèvres, les parties extérie: An anomaly of the labia, the externally visible portions of the vulva
organes génitaux ambigus chez un individ: Ambiguous genitalia in an individual with XX genetic gender
phénotype génital qui ne peut être clair: A genital phenotype that is not clearly assignable to a single gender. Ambiguous genitalia can be evaluated using the Prader scale: Prader 0: Normal female external genitalia. Prader 1: Female external genitalia with clitoromegaly. Prader 2: Clitoromegaly with partial labial fusion forming a funnel-shaped urogenital sinus. Prader 3: Increased phallic enlargement. Complete labioscrotal fusion forming a urogenital sinus with a single opening. Prader 4: Complete scrotal fusion with urogenital opening at the base or on the shaft of the phallus. Prader 5: Normal male external genitalia. The diagnosis of ambiguous genitalia is made for Prader 1-4
fusion des petites lèvres à la suite d'a: Fusion of the labia minora as a result of labial adhesions resulting in vaginal obstruction
anomalie congénitale caractérisée par la: Congenital anomaly characterized by closure or failure to develop an opening in the urethra in females
Congenital anomaly characterized by closure or failure to develop an opening in the urethra
l'urétérocèle est une dilatation saccula: A ureterocele is a congenital saccular dilatation of the distal segment of the ureter
la présence d'un uretère sténosé, c'est-: The presence of a stenotic, i.e., constricted ureter
The distention of the ureter with urine
anomalie du développement caractérisée p: A developmental anomaly characterized by the presence of two, instead of one, ureter connecting a kidney to the bladder
blocage de l'écoulement de l'urine du ba: Blockage of urine flow from the renal pelvis to the proximal ureter
anomalie congénitale des voies urinaires: A congenital anomaly of the urinary tract, in which the kidney is duplicated and is drained via two separate renal pelves and ureters
mouvement anormal (rétrograde) de l'urin: Abnormal (retrograde) movement of urine from the bladder into ureters or kidneys related to inadequacy of the valvular mechanism at the ureterovesicular junction or other causes
une duplication du système collecteur du: A duplication of the collecting system of the kidney, defined as a kidney with two (instead of, normally, one) pyelocaliceal systems. The pyelocaliceal system is comprised of the renal pelvis and calices. The duplicated renal collecting system can be associated with a single ureter or with double ureters. In the latter case, the two ureters empty separately into the bladder or fuse to form a single ureteral orifice
réduction du niveau de performance des r: A reduction in the level of performance of the kidneys in areas of function comprising the concentration of urine, removal of wastes, the maintenance of electrolyte balance, homeostasis of blood pressure, and calcium metabolism
connexion entre le rein droit et le rein: A connection of the right and left kidney by an isthmus of functioning renal parenchyma or fibrous tissue that crosses the midline
défaut de développement dans lequel un r: A developmental defect in which a kidney is located in an abnormal anatomic position
la présence de tubules rénaux avec des m: The presence of renal tubules with thick redundant basement membranes, or a reduction of greater than 50% in tubular diameter compared to surrounding non-atrophic tubules
accumulation de tissu cicatriciel dans l: Accumulation of scar tissue within the glomerulus
accumulation segmentaire de tissu cicatr: Segmental accumulation of scar tissue in individual (but not all) glomeruli
le syndrome néphrotique est un ensemble: Nephrotic syndrome is a collection of findings resulting from glomerular dysfunction with an increase in glomerular capillary wall permeability associated with pronounced proteinuria. Nephrotic syndrome refers to the constellation of clinical findings that result from severe renal loss of protein, with Proteinuria and hypoalbuminemia, edema, and hyperlipidemia
la présence d'une dysplasie lors du déve: The presence of developmental dysplasia of the kidney
augmentation du nombre et de la taille d: Increased number and size of the juxtaglomerular cells
terme non spécifique désignant une malad: A nonspecific term referring to disease or damage of the kidneys
dysfonctionnement du tubule proximal, qu: Dysfunction of the proximal tubule, which is the portion of the duct system of the nephron of the kidney which leads from Bowman's capsule to the loop of Henle
phosphates urinaires élevés en présence: High urine phosphate in the presence of hypophosphatemia
la présence de toute anomalie du système: The presence of any abnormality of the genitourinary system
la néphrocalcinose est le dépôt de sels: Nephrocalcinosis is the deposition of calcium salts in renal parenchyma
distension sévère du rein avec dilatatio: Severe distention of the kidney with dilation of the renal pelvis and calices
présence d'un gonadoblastome, un néoplas: The presence of a gonadoblastoma, a neoplasm of a gonad that consists of aggregates of germ cells and sex cord elements
distance entre les commissures orales su: Distance between the oral commissures more than 2 SD above the mean. Alternatively, an apparently increased width of the oral aperture (subjective)
érosion de la muqueuse buccale avec exca: Erosion of the mucous mebrane of the mouth with local excavation of the surface, resulting from the sloughing of inflammatory necrotic tissue
distance entre les commissures buccales: Distance between the commissures of the mouth more than 2 SD below the mean. Alternatively, an apparently decreased width of the oral aperture (subjective)
type de fente labiale se présentant sous: A type of cleft lip presenting as a midline (median) gap in the upper lip
anomalie de la cavité buccale, c'est-à-d: Abnormality of the oral cavity, i.e., the opening or hollow part of the mouth
toute anomalie de la gencive: Any abnormality of the gingiva (also known as gums)
anomalie de la luette, la projection con: Abnormality of the uvula, the conic projection from the posterior edge of the middle of the soft palate
toute anomalie du palais, c'est-à-dire d: Any abnormality of the palate, i.e., of roof of the mouth
la fente palatine est un défaut de dével: Cleft palate is a developmental defect of the palate resulting from a failure of fusion of the palatine processes and manifesting as a separation of the roof of the mouth (soft and hard palate)
les fentes sous-muqueuses du palais dur: Hard-palate submucous clefts are characterized by bony defects in the midline of the bony palate that are covered by the mucous membrane of the roof of the mouth. It may be possible to detect a submucous cleft hard palate upon palpation as a notch in the bony palate
épaisseur accrue de la lèvre inférieure,: Increased thickness of the lower lip, leading to a prominent appearance of the lower lip. The height of the vermilion of the lower lip in the midline is more than 2 SD above the mean. Alternatively, an apparently increased height of the vermilion of the lower lip in the frontal view (subjective)
multiples indentations et/ou élévations: Multiple indentations and/or elevations on the edge and/or surface of the tongue producing an irregular surface contour
fente du palais mou (également appelée v: Cleft of the soft palate (also known as the velum, or muscular palate) as a result of a developmental defect occurring between the 7th and 12th week of pregnancy. Cleft soft palate can cause functional abnormalities of the Eustachian tube with resulting middle ear anomalies and hearing difficulties, as well as speech problems associated with hypernasal speech due to velopharyngeal insufficiency
largeur du palais supérieure à 2 ds en d: Width of the palate more than 2 SD below the mean (objective) or apparently decreased palatal width (subjective)
anomalie du frein lingual, c'est-à-dire: An abnormality of the lingual frenulum, that is of the small fold of mucous membrane that attaches the tongue to the floor of the mouth, or the presence of accessory frenula in the oral cavity
pli supplémentaire de tissu s'étendant d: Extra fold of tissue extending from the alveolar ridge to the inner surface of the upper or lower lip
uvule séparée en deux parties, plus faci: Uvula separated into two parts most easily seen at the tip
aspect du visage caractérisé par une ouv: A facial appearance characterized by a permanently or nearly permanently opened mouth
dépression située sur le vermillon de la: Depression located on the vermilion of the lower lip, usually paramedian
toute anomalie des glandes parotides, qu: Any abnormality of the parotid glands, which are the salivary glands that are located in the subcutaneous tissues of the face overlying the mandibular ramus and anterior and inferior to the external ear
la présence d'un créneau lingual anormal: The presence of an abnormally short lingual frenulum
la présence d'une fente dans la cavité b: The presence of a cleft in the oral cavity, the two main types of which are cleft lip and cleft palate. In cleft lip, there is the congenital failure of the maxillary and median nasal processes to fuse, forming a groove or fissure in the lip. In cleft palate, there is a congenital failure of the palate to fuse properly, forming a grooved depression or fissure in the roof of the mouth. Clefts of the lip and palate can occur individually or together. It is preferable to code each defect separately
une fente de la lèvre supérieure. il s'a: A gap in the upper lip. This is a congenital defect resulting from nonfusion of tissues of the lip during embryonal development
une anomalie de l'apparence du visage ca: An abnormality of the appearance of the face caused by constant contraction of the lips leading to a puckered or pursed appearance
la présence d'une forme triangulaire de: The presence of a triangular form of the mouth
limitation de la capacité à ouvrir la bo: Limitation in the ability to open the mouth
hyperplasie de la gencive (c'est-à-dire: Hyperplasia of the gingiva (that is, a thickening of the soft tissue overlying the alveolar ridge. The degree of thickening ranges from involvement of the interdental papillae alone to gingival overgrowth covering the entire tooth crown
télangiectasie (c'est-à-dire la présence: Telangiectasia (that is, the presence of small dilated superficial blood vessels) of the lips
hauteur du vermillon de la lèvre supérie: Height of the vermilion of the upper lip in the midline more than 2 SD above the mean. Alternatively, an apparently increased height of the vermilion of the upper lip in the frontal view (subjective)
sécheresse de la bouche due à un dysfonc: Dryness of the mouth due to salivary gland dysfunction
hauteur du palais supérieure à 2 ds au-d: Height of the palate more than 2 SD above the mean (objective) or palatal height at the level of the first permanent molar more than twice the height of the teeth (subjective)
hauteur du vermillon de la lèvre supérie: Height of the vermilion of the upper lip in the midline more than 2 SD below the mean. Alternatively, an apparently reduced height of the vermilion of the upper lip in the frontal view (subjective)
incapacité du sphincter vélopharyngien à: Inability of velopharyngeal sphincter to sufficiently separate the nasal cavity from the oral cavity during speech
accentuation des sillons sur la face dor: Accentuation of the grooves on the dorsal surface of the tongue
télangiectasie (c'est-à-dire présence de: Telangiectasia (that is, the presence of small dilated superficial blood vessels) of the tongue
présence de télangiectasies dans la cavi: Presence of telangiectases in the oral cavity
configuration anormale de la lèvre infér: An abnormal configuration of the lower lip such that it is turned outward i.e., everted, with the Inner aspect of the lower lip vermilion (normally opposing the teeth) being visible in a frontal view
largeur réduite de la peau de la région: Reduced width of the skin of vermilion border region of upper lip
toute anomalie des fontanelles (régions: Any abnormality of the fontanelles (the regions covered by a thick membrane that normally ossify in the first two years of life) or the cranial sutures (the fibrous joints in which the articulating bones or cartilages of the skull are connected by sutural ligaments )
une anomalie de la fontanelle antérieure: An abnormality of the anterior fontanelle, i.e., the cranial fontanelle that is located at the intersection of the coronal and sagittal sutures
diminution anormale de la taille de la f: Abnormally decreased size of the anterior fontanelle with respect to age-dependent norms
l'hydrocéphalie est une distension activ: Hydrocephalus is an active distension of the ventricular system of the brain resulting from inadequate passage of CSF from its point of production within the cerebral ventricles to its point of absorption into the systemic circulation
chez les nouveau-nés, les deux os fronta: In newborns, the two frontal bones, two parietal bones, and one occipital bone are joined by fibrous sutures, which form a small posterior fontanelle, and a larger, diamond-shaped anterior fontanelle. These regions allow for the skull to pass the birth canal and for later growth. The fontanelles gradually ossify, whereby the posterior fontanelle usually closes by eight weeks and the anterior fontanelle by the 9th to 16th month of age. Large fontanelles are diagnosed if the fontanelles are larger than age-dependent norms
Any abnormality of the size of the skull
la bosse pariétale est une proéminence m: Parietal bossing is a marked prominence in the parietal region
tête cunéiforme ou triangulaire, avec le: Wedge-shaped, or triangular head, with the apex of the triangle at the midline of the forehead and the base of the triangle at the occiput
hauteur verticale anormale du crâne et r: Abnormal vertical height of the skull and a shortening of its anterior-posterior length, frequently combined with malformations of the occipital region
anomalie des sinus paranasaux (crâniens): Abnormality of the paranasal (cranial) sinuses, which are air-filled spaces that are located within the bones of the skull and face and communicate with the nasal cavity. They comprise the maxillary sinuses, the frontal sinuses, the ethmoid sinuses, and the sphenoid sinuses
inflammation des sinus paranasaux due à: Inflammation of the paranasal sinuses owing to a viral, bacterial, or fungal infection, allergy, or an autoimmune reaction
anomalie de la forme du crâne caractéris: An abnormality of skull shape characterized by a decreased anterior-posterior diameter. That is, a cephalic index greater than 81%. Alternatively, an apparently shortened anteroposterior dimension (length) of the head compared to width
une augmentation anormale de la densité: An abnormal increase of density of the bones making up the calvaria
circonférence occipito-frontale (tête) i: Occipito-frontal (head) circumference (OFC) less than -3 standard deviations compared to appropriate, age matched, normal standards (Ross JJ, Frias JL 1977, PMID:9683597). Alternatively, decreased size of the cranium
une microcéphalie progressive est diagno: Progressive microcephaly is diagnosed when the head circumference falls progressively behind age- and gender-dependent norms
circonférence occipitofrontale (de la tê: Occipitofrontal (head) circumference greater than 97th centile compared to appropriate, age matched, sex-matched normal standards. Alternatively, a apparently increased size of the cranium
élargissement de la fontanelle antérieur: Enlargement of the anterior fontanelle with respect to age-dependent norms
tête haute par rapport à la largeur et à: Tall head relative to width and length
l'oxycéphalie (du grec oxus, pointu, et: Oxycephaly (from Greek oxus, sharp, and kephalos, head) refers to a conical or pointed shape of the skull
anomalie de l'apophyse mastoïde, qui est: An abnormality of the mastoid process, which is the conical prominence projecting from the undersurface of the mastoid portion of the temporal bone
une anomalie de la forme du crâne caract: An abnormality of skull shape characterized by a increased anterior-posterior diameter, i.e., an increased antero-posterior dimension of the skull. Cephalic index less than 76%. Alternatively, an apparently increased antero-posterior length of the head compared to width. Often due to premature closure of the sagittal suture
augmentation de la convexité de l'occipu: Increased convexity of the occiput (posterior part of the skull)
les nourrissons ont normalement deux fon: Infants normally have two fontanels at birth, the diamond-shaped anterior fontanelle at the junction of the coronal and sagittal sutures, and the posterior fontanelle at the intersection of the occipital and parietal bones. The posterior fontanelle usually closes by the 8th week of life, and the anterior fontanel closes by the 18th month of life on average. This term applies if there is delay of closure of the fontanelles beyond the normal age
sous-développement de la proéminence mal: Underdevelopment of the malar prominence of the jugal bone (zygomatic bone in mammals), appreciated in profile, frontal view, and/or by palpation
une face courte (hp:0011219) et étroite: A face that is short (HP:0011219) and narrow (HP:0000275)
la largeur des os bizygomatiques (partie: Bizygomatic (upper face) and bigonial (lower face) width are both more than 2 standard deviations below the mean (objective); or, an apparent reduction in the width of the upper and lower face (subjective)
la hauteur (longueur) du visage est supé: Facial height (length) is more than 2 standard deviations above the mean (objective); or, an apparent increase in the height (length) of the face (subjective)
toute anomalie de la mandibule, l'os de: Any abnormality of the mandible, the bone of the lower jaw
anomalie dans laquelle la mandibule est: An abnormality in which the mandible is mislocalised posteriorly
absence d'aspect fin et net des sourcils: Absence of fine and sharp appearance of brows, nose, lips, mouth, and chin, usually because of rounded and heavy features or thickened skin with or without thickening of subcutaneous and bony tissues
largeur bizygomatique (haut du visage) e: Bizygomatic (upper face) and bigonial (lower face) width greater than 2 standard deviations above the mean (objective); or an apparent increase in the width of the face (subjective)
un pli de peau commençant au-dessus de l: A fold of skin starting above the medial aspect of the upper eyelid and arching downward to cover, pass in front of and lateral to the medial canthus
une quantité accrue de tissu adipeux sou: An increased amount of subcutaneous fat tissue in the face
distance entre les crêtes du philtrum, m: Distance between the philtral ridges, measured just above the vermilion border, more than 2 standard deviations above the mean, or alternatively, an apparently increased distance between the ridges of the philtrum
perte de tissu graisseux sous-cutané nor: Loss of normal subcutaneous fat tissue in the face
proéminence ou rondeur accrue des tissus: Increased prominence or roundness of soft tissues between zygomata and mandible
distance entre la ligne des cheveux (tri: Distance between the hairline (trichion) and the glabella (the most prominent point on the frontal bone above the root of the nose), in the midline, more than two SD below the mean. Alternatively, an apparently decreased distance between the hairline and the glabella
une apparence faciale caractéristique av: A characteristic facial appearance with a round facial form, full cheeks, a short nose, and a relatively small chin
une diminution du tonus musculaire d'un: Reduced muscle tone of a muscle that is innervated by the facial nerve (the seventh cranial nerve)
un manque d'expression faciale, avec sou: A lack of facial expression often with staring eyes and a slightly open mouth
un visage au contour arrondi et légèreme: A face with a rounded and slightly elongated outline
anomalie d'un muscle innervé par le nerf: An anomaly of a muscle that is innervated by the facial nerve (the seventh cranial nerve)
une proéminence anormale du menton liée: Abnormal prominence of the chin related to increased length of the mandible
anomalie du menton, c'est-à-dire de la p: An abnormality of the chin, i.e., of the inferior portion of the face lying inferior to the lower lip and including the central prominence of the lower jaw
un rétrécissement marqué de la partie in: A marked tapering of the lower face to the chin
une forme d'hypoplasie développementale: A form of developmental hypoplasia of the mandible in which the mandible is mislocalised posteriorly
une anomalie de la face moyenne, qui est: An anomaly of the midface, which is a region and not an anatomical term. It extends, superiorly, from the inferior orbital margin to, inferiorly, the level of nasal base. It is formed by the maxilla (upper jaw) and zygoma and cheeks and malar region. Traditionally, the nose and premaxilla are not included in the midface
l'aspect du visage, vu de face, est plus: The facial appearance is more circular than usual as viewed from the front
la distance interpupillaire est supérieu: Interpupillary distance more than 2 SD above the mean (alternatively, the appearance of an increased interpupillary distance or widely spaced eyes)
la myokymie faciale est une activité fib: Facial myokymia is a fine fibrillary activity of one or more muscles innervated by the facial nerve (the seventh cranial nerve)
surface cutanée plate, sans formation de: Flat skin surface, with no ridge formation in the central region of the upper lip between the nasal base and upper vermilion border
les contours du visage, vus de face, mon: Facial contours, as viewed from the front, show a broad upper face/cranium and lower face/mandible, creating a square appearance
distance entre la base nasale et le bord: Distance between nasal base and midline upper lip vermilion border more than 2 SD below the mean. Alternatively, an apparently decreased distance between nasal base and midline upper lip vermilion border
une différence anormale entre les côtés: An abnormal difference between the left and right sides of the face
contour du visage, vu de face, de forme: Facial contour, as viewed from the front, triangular in shape, with breadth at the temples and tapering to a narrow chin
anomalie du maxillaire (os de la mâchoir: An abnormality of the Maxilla (upper jaw bone)
dimension anormalement petite du maxilla: Abnormally small dimension of the Maxilla. Usually creating a malocclusion or malalignment between the upper and lower teeth or resulting in a deficient amount of projection of the base of the nose and lower midface region
hémangiome, une tumeur bénigne des cellu: Hemangioma, a benign tumor of the vascular endothelial cells, occurring in the face
diminution de la distance verticale entr: Decreased vertical distance from the vermilion border of the lower lip to the inferior-most point of the chin
projection avant et/ou latérale de la pa: Greater than average forward and/or lateral protrusion of the supraorbital portion of the frontal bones
largeur du front ou distance entre les f: Width of the forehead or distance between the frontotemporales is more than two standard deviations above the mean (objective); or apparently increased distance between the two sides of the forehead
un degré réduit de mouvement des muscles: A reduced degree of motion of the muscles beneath the skin of the face, often associated with reduced facial crease formation
caractéristiques faciales grossières rap: Coarse facial features reminiscent of those of a boxer
inclinaison de la surface antérieure du: Inclination of the anterior surface of the forehead from the vertical more than two standard deviations above the mean (objective); or apparently excessive posterior sloping of the forehead in a lateral view
largeur du front ou distance entre les f: Width of the forehead or distance between the frontotemporales is more than two standard deviations below the mean (objective); or apparently narrow intertemporal region (subjective)
distance entre la base du nez et le bord: Distance between nasal base and midline upper lip vermilion border more than 2 SD above the mean. Alternatively, an apparently increased distance between nasal base and midline upper lip vermilion border
une anomalie de la morphologie faciale c: An abnormality of facial morphology characterized by a small mouth opening and constant contraction of the lips as if the patient were whistling
une hauteur anormalement élevée du front: An abnormally increased height of the forehead
ligne frontale des cheveux avec des arcs: Frontal hairline with bilateral arcs to a low point in the midline of the forehead
présence d'un front anormalement petit: The presence of a forehead that is abnormally small
un type de localisation anormale des ore: A type of abnormal location of the ears in which the position of the ears is characterized by posterior rotation (the superior part of the ears is rotated towards the back of the head, and the inferior part of the ears towards the front)
un acouphène est une perception auditive: Tinnitus is an auditory perception that can be described as the experience of sound, in the ear or in the head, in the absence of external acoustic stimulation
dans l'otospongiose, un cal osseux s'acc: In otosclerosis, a callus of bone accumulates on the stapes creating a partial fixation. This limits the movement of the stapes bone, which results in hearing loss
une anomalie du lobule du pavillon de l': An abnormality of the lobule of pinna
une anomalie de la perception sensoriell: An abnormality of the sensory perception of sound
une diminution de l'amplitude de la perc: A decreased magnitude of the sensory perception of sound
oreilles implantées bas (hp:0000369) et: Ears that are low-set (HP:0000369) and posteriorly rotated (HP:0000358)
insertion supérieure de l'oreille au cui: Upper insertion of the ear to the scalp below an imaginary horizontal line drawn between the inner canthi of the eye and extending posteriorly to the ear
l'otite moyenne aiguë est une infection: Acute otitis media is a short and generally painful infection of the middle ear
dans le cas d'une partition ii incomplèt: IWith incomplete partition II, the cochlea consists of 1.5 turns; the apical and middle cochlea turns are undifferentiated and form a cystic apex. The vestibule is normal while the vestibular aqueduct is always enlarged. Developmental arrest occurs at the seventh week of gestation
anomalie du pavillon de l'oreille, égale: An abnormality of the pinna, which is also referred to as the auricle or external ear
oreille saillante latéralement et dépour: Laterally protruding ear that lacks antihelical folding (including absence of inferior and superior crura)
l'ankylose de l'étrier désigne la fixati: Stapes ankylosis refers to congenital or acquired fixation of the stapes (the stirrup-shaped small bone or ossicle in the middle ear), which is associated with conductive hearing resulting from impairment of the sound-conduction mechanism (the external auditory canal, tympanic membrane, and/or middle-ear ossicles)
étiquette rudimentaire de peau contenant: A rudimentary tag of skin often containing ear tissue including a core of cartilage and located just anterior to the auricle (outer part of the ear)
absence de tissu charnu non cartilagineu: Absence of fleshy non-cartilaginous tissue inferior to the tragus and incisura
inflammation ou infection de l'oreille m: Inflammation or infection of the middle ear
l'otite moyenne chronique désigne un liq: Chronic otitis media refers to fluid, swelling, or infection of the middle ear that does not heal and may cause permanent damage to the ear
pliage antérieur et inférieur de la part: Anterior and inferior folding of the upper portion of the ear that obliterates triangular fossa and scapha
la présence d'un anthélix anormalement p: The presence of an abnormally prominent antihelix
état dans lequel l'hélix est replié à un: A condition in which the helix is folded over to a greater degree than normal. That is, excessive curling of the helix edge, whereby the free edge is parallel to the plane of the ear
forme de surdité de perception d'origine: A form of sensorineural deafness with either congenital onset or infantile onset, i.e., before the acquisition of speech
longueur longitudinale médiane de l'orei: Median longitudinal ear length greater than two standard deviations above the mean and median ear width greater than two standard deviations above the mean (objective); or, apparent increase in length and width of the pinna (subjective)
un rétrécissement anormal du conduit aud: An abnormal narrowing of the external auditory canal
une susceptibilité accrue à l'otite moye: Increased susceptibility to otitis media, as manifested by recurrent episodes of otitis media
anomalie de la conduction vibratoire du: An abnormality of vibrational conductance of sound to the inner ear leading to impairment of sensory perception of sound
type de déficience auditive dans une ou: A type of hearing impairment in one or both ears related to an abnormal functionality of the cochlear nerve
une forme progressive de déficience audi: A progressive form of sensorineural hearing impairment
type de perte auditive résultant de la c: A type of hearing loss resulting from a combination of conductive hearing impairment and sensorineural hearing impairment
angle formé par le plan de l'oreille et: Angle formed by the plane of the ear and the mastoid bone greater than the 97th centile for age (objective); or, outer edge of the helix more than 2 cm from the mastoid at the point of maximum distance (objective)
absence ou défaut de formation du condui: Absence or failure to form of the external auditory canal
augmentation du volume et forme globulai: Increased volume and globular shape of the anteroinferior aspect of the nose
anomalie des choanes (les ouvertures nas: Abnormality of the choanae (the posterior nasal apertures)
réduction de la longueur supérieure/infé: Reduced superior to inferior length of the nasal septum
un épistaxis, ou saignement de nez, dési: Epistaxis, or nosebleed, refers to a hemorrhage localized in the nose
anomalie de l'arête nasale, qui est la z: Abnormality of the nasal bridge, which is the saddle-shaped area that includes the nasal root and the lateral aspects of the nose. It lies between the glabella and the inferior boundary of the nasal bone, and extends laterally to the inner canthi
positionnement antérieur de la racine na: Anterior positioning of the nasal root in comparison to the usual positioning for age
une anomalie de l'aile du nez: An abnormality of the Ala of nose
aile du nez amincie, déficiente ou exces: Thinned, deficient, or excessively arched ala nasi
augmentation de la largeur de l'arête na: Increased breadth of the nasal bridge (and with it, the nasal root)
diminution de la distance entre l'extrém: Decreased distance from the nasal tip to the nasal base
crête nasale s'incurvant vers l'avant ju: Nasal ridge curving anteriorly to an imaginary line that connects the nasal root and tip. The nose appears often also prominent, and the columella low
distance interalaire supérieure de plus: Interalar distance more than two standard deviations above the mean for age, i.e., an apparently increased width of the nasal base and alae
diminution de la largeur de l'arête nasa: Decreased width of the bony bridge of the nose
distance entre les régions subnasale et: Distance between subnasale and pronasale more than two standard deviations above the mean, or alternatively, an apparently increased anterior protrusion of the nasal tip
rétrécissement anormal des choanes (l'ou: Abnormal narrowing of the choana (the posterior nasal aperture)
absence ou fermeture anormale des choane: Absence or abnormal closure of the choana (the posterior nasal aperture)
augmentation de la largeur de la pointe: Increase in width of the nasal tip
une scission de la pointe nasale. indent: A splitting of the nasal tip. Visually assessable vertical indentation, cleft, or depression of the nasal tip
manque de proéminence du nez résultant d: Lack of prominence of the nose resulting from a posteriorly-placed nasal ridge
une incapacité à percevoir les odeurs. i: An inability to perceive odors. This is a general term describing inability to smell arising in any part of the process of smelling from absorption of odorants into the nasal mucous overlying the olfactory epithelium, diffusion to the cilia, binding to olfactory receptor sites, generation of action potentials in olfactory neurons, and perception of a smell
distance interalaire inférieure de plus: Interalar distance more than 2 SD below the mean for age, or alternatively, an apparently decreased width of the nasal base and alae
les narines orientées vers l'avant, vues: Anteriorly-facing nostrils viewed with the head in the Frankfurt horizontal and the eyes of the observer level with the eyes of the subject. This gives the appearance of an upturned nose (upturned nasal tip)
le pterygium colli est un pli cutané con: Pterygium colli is a congenital skin fold that runs along the sides of the neck down to the shoulders. It involves an ectopic fibrotic facial band superficial to the trapezius muscle. Excess hair-bearing skin is also present and extends down the cervical region well beyond the normal hairline
une diminution de la force de la muscula: Decreased strength of the neck musculature
une quantité accrue de tissu adipeux sou: An increased amount of subcutaneous fat tissue around the neck
dysplasie affectant la vascularisation d: Dysplasia affecting the vasculature of the gastrointestinal tract
augmentation de la longueur inféro-supér: Increased inferior-superior length of the neck
contractions involontaires de la muscula: Involuntary contractions of the neck musculature resulting in an abnormal posture of or abnormal movements of the head
un épaississement de l'épaisseur de la p: A thickening of the skin thickness in the posterior aspect of the fetal neck. A nuchal fold measurement is obtained in a transverse section of the fetal head at the level of the cavum septum pellucidum and thalami, angled posteriorly to include the cerebellum. The measurement is taken from the outer edge of the occiput bone to the outer skin limit directly in the midline. A measurement 6 mm or more is considered significant between 18 and 24 weeks and a measurement of 5 mm or more is considered significant at 16 to 18 weeks (pmid:16100637)
augmentation de la largeur du cou: Increased side-to-side width of the neck
lésion lymphatique kystique du cou: A cystic lymphatic lesion of the neck
toute anomalie de l'œil, y compris l'emp: Any abnormality of the eye, including location, spacing, and intraocular abnormalities
entaille ou fente de la rétine: A notch or cleft of the retina
toute anomalie de la cornée, qui est le: Any abnormality of the cornea, which is the transparent tissue at the front of the eye that covers the iris, pupil, and anterior chamber
anomalie congénitale de la cornée : la c: A congenital abnormality of the cornea in which the cornea and the anterior segment of the eye are smaller than normal. The horizontal diameter of the cornea does not reach 10 mm even in adulthood
un type d'astigmatisme associé à des cou: A type of astigmatism associated with abnormal curvatures on the anterior and/or posterior surface of the cornea
forme d'astigmatisme dans laquelle un mé: A form of astigmatism in which one meridian is hyperopic while the one at a right angle to it has no refractive error
un élargissement de la cornée avec une c: An enlargement of the cornea with normal clarity and function. Megalocornea is diagnosed with a horizontal corneal diameter of 12 mm or more at birth or 13 mm or more after two years of age
un défaut d'alignement des yeux tel que: A misalignment of the eyes so that the visual axes deviate from bifoveal fixation. The classification of strabismus may be based on a number of features including the relative position of the eyes, whether the deviation is latent or manifest, intermittent or constant, concomitant or otherwise and according to the age of onset and the relevance of any associated refractive error
toute maladie non inflammatoire de la ré: Any noninflammatory disease of the retina. This nonspecific term is retained here because of its wide use in the literature, but if possible new annotations should indicate the precise type of retinal abnormality
un œil plus profondément enfoncé dans le: An eye that is more deeply recessed into the plane of the face than is typical
une anomalie de la fovéa centrale, la zo: An abnormality of the fovea centralis, the central area of the macula that mediates central, high resolution vision and contains the largest concentration of cone cells in the retina
l'inclinaison de la fissure palpébrale e: The palpebral fissure inclination is more than two standard deviations below the mean
la présence d'érosions épithéliales corn: The presence of recurrent corneal epithelial erosions. Although most corneal epithelial defects heal quickly, some may show recurrent ulcerations
une anomalie des mouvements oculaires vo: An abnormality in voluntary or involuntary eye movements or their control
le glaucome entraine une perte de cellul: Glaucoma refers loss of retinal ganglion cells in a characteristic pattern of optic neuropathy usually associated with increased intraocular pressure
présence d'un nombre accru de torsions e: The presence of an increased number of twists and turns of the conjunctival blood vessels
la déficience visuelle (ou trouble de la: Visual impairment (or vision impairment) is vision loss (of a person) to such a degree as to qualify as an additional support need through a significant limitation of visual capability resulting from either disease, trauma, or congenital or degenerative conditions that cannot be corrected by conventional means, such as refractive correction, medication, or surgery
distance entre les canthi internes supér: Distance between the inner canthi more than two standard deviations above the mean (objective); or, apparently increased distance between the inner canthi
le bord de la paupière supérieure est po: The upper eyelid margin is positioned 3 mm or more lower than usual and covers the superior portion of the iris (objective); or, the upper lid margin obscures at least part of the pupil (subjective)
un sous-type de maladie rétinienne héréd: An inherited retinal disease subtype in which the rod photoreceptors appear to be more severely affected than the cone photoreceptors. Typical presentation is with nyctalopia (due to rod dysfunction) followed by loss of mid-peripheral field of vision, which gradually extends and leaves many patients with a small central island of vision due to the preservation of macular cones
la paralysie supranucléaire du regard es: A supranuclear gaze palsy is an inability to look in a vertical direction as a result of cerebral impairment. There is a loss of the voluntary aspect of eye movements, but, as the brainstem is still intact, all the reflex conjugate eye movements are normal
toute anomalie de réponse électrique des: Any abnormality of the electrical responses of various cell types in the retina as measured by electroretinography
une vitesse anormalement lente des mouve: An abnormally slow velocity of the saccadic eye movements
une cataracte est une opacité ou un trou: A cataract is an opacity or clouding that develops in the crystalline lens of the eye or in its capsule
cataracte congénitale résultant d'un déf: A cataract that occurs congenitally as the result of a developmental defect, in contrast to the majority of cataracts that occur in adulthood as the result of degenerative changes of the lens
un œil qui fait saillie vers l'avant par: An eye that is protruding anterior to the plane of the face to a greater extent than is typical
une cataracte qui affecte la région du c: A cataract that affects the region of the lens directly beneath the capsule of the lens
présence de petits vaisseaux sanguins di: The presence of small (ca. 0.5-1.0 mm) dilated blood vessels near the surface of the mucous membranes of the conjunctiva
une anomalie de l'iris, tissu musculaire: An abnormality of the iris, which is the pigmented muscular tissue between the cornea and the lens, that is perforated by an opening called the pupil
anomalie de l'iris caractérisée par une: Abnormality of the iris characterized by, typically bilateral, complete or partial iris hypoplasia. The phenotype ranges from mild defects of anterior iris stroma only to almost complete absence of the iris
longueur moyenne des cils supérieurs > 1: Mid upper eyelash length >10 mm or increased length of the eyelashes (subjective)
absence de globe oculaire: Absence of the globe or eyeball
diminution de l'acuité visuelle précédem: A reduction of previously attained ability to see
atrophie de la choroïde et des couches r: Atrophy of the choroid and retinal layers of the fundus
diminution de la densité/du nombre et/ou: Decreased density/number and/or decreased diameter of eyebrow hairs
pli de peau commençant au niveau de la f: A fold of skin starting at or just below the medial aspect of the lower lid and arching upward to cover, extend in front of and lateral to the medial canthus
disque optique apparent en l'absence d'h: Apparent optic disc swelling in the absence of increased intracranial pressure
anomalie dans le processus de focalisati: An abnormality in the process of focusing of light by the eye in order to produce a sharp image on the retina
anomalie de la réfraction caractérisée p: An abnormality of refraction characterized by the ability to see objects in the distance clearly, while objects nearby appear blurry
séparation des couches internes de la ré: Separation of the inner layers of the retina (neural retina) from the pigment epithelium
diminution de la capacité à déplacer l'œ: Reduced ability to move the eye in the direction of the nose
une coloration jaune pâle du disque opti: A pale yellow discoloration of the optic disk (the area of the optic nerve head in the retina). The optic disc normally has a pinkish hue with a central yellowish depression
anomalie de la réfraction caractérisée p: An abnormality of refraction characterized by the ability to see objects nearby clearly, while objects in the distance appear blurry
terme non spécifique désignant la dégéné: A nonspecific term denoting degeneration of the retinal pigment epithelium and/or retinal photoreceptor cells
tout écart par rapport à la coordination: Any deviation from the normal motor coordination of the eyes that allows for bilateral fixation on a single object
absence de réponse à la stimulation lors: Lack of any response to stimulation upon electroretinography
anomalie de la capacité à distinguer ou: An anomaly in the ability to discriminate between or recognize colors
difficulté à distinguer le jaune du bleu: Difficulty distinguishing between yellow and blue, possible related to dysfunction of the S photopigment
une anomalie de l'uvée, la couche vascul: An abnormality of the uvea, the vascular layer of the eyeball
inflammation d'une ou de toutes les part: Inflammation of one or all portions of the uveal tract
un reflet blanc anormal de la pupille au: An abnormal white reflection from the pupil rather than the usual black reflection
la dystrophie rétinienne est une anomali: Retinal dystrophy is an abnormality of the retina associated with a hereditary process. Retinal dystrophies are defined by their predominantly monogenic inheritance and they are frequently associated with loss or dysfunction of photoreceptor cells as a primary or secondary event
œil de grande taille diffuse (avec mégal: Diffusely large eye (with megalocornea) associated with glaucoma
malformation congénitale du segment anté: A congenital malformation of the anterior segment characterized by iridicorneal malformation, glaucoma, iris stroma hypoplasia, posterior embryotoxon, and corneal opacities
déformation de la cornée en forme de côn: A cone-shaped deformity of the cornea characterized by the presence of corneal distortion secondary to thinning of the apex
trouble du développement du système de d: A developmental disorder of the lacrimal drainage system that most often affects the lacrimal ostium and resulting in non-opening of the nasolacrimal duct. It usually results from a non-canalization of the nasolacrimal duct
une forme de strabisme avec un ou les de: A form of strabismus with one or both eyes turned inward ('crossed') to a relatively severe degree, usually defined as 10 diopters or more
absence d'une région de la rétine, de l': Absence of a region of the retina, retinal pigment epithelium, and choroid
anomalie du développement caractérisée p: A developmental anomaly characterized by abnormal smallness of one or both eyes
anomalie des mouvements oculaires caract: An abnormality of eye movement characterized by impairment of fast (saccadic) eye movements
un mouvement oculaire saccadique avec mo: Saccadic undershoot, i.e., a saccadic eye movement that has less than the magnitude that would be required to gain fixation of the object
perte d'acuité visuelle (impliquant que: Loss of visual acuity (implying that vision was better at a certain timepoint in life). Otherwise the term reduced visual acuity should be used (or a subclass of that)
augmentation de la densité/du nombre/du: Increased density/number and/or increased diameter of eyebrow hairs
une augmentation régionale et pathologiq: A regional and pathological increase of the light detection threshold in any region of the visual field surrounded by a field of normal or relatively well-preserved vision
un scotome (zone de vision réduite dans: A scotoma (area of diminished vision within the visual field) located between the central point of fixation and the blind spot with a roughly horizontal oval shape
une forme de strabisme avec un ou les de: A form of strabismus with one or both eyes deviated outward
anomalie de la rétine caractérisée par u: An abnormality of the retina characterized by pigment deposition. It is typically associated with migration and proliferation of macrophages or retinal pigment epithelial cells into the retina; melanin from these cells causes the pigmentary changes. Pigmentary retinopathy is a common final pathway of many retinal conditions and is often associated with visual loss
une réduction fixe de la distance vertic: A fixed reduction in the vertical distance between the upper and lower eyelids with short palpebral fissures
l'inclinaison de la fissure palpébrale e: The palpebral fissure inclination is more than two standard deviations above the mean for age (objective); or, the inclination of the palpebral fissure is greater than typical for age
anomalie de la cornée caractérisée par u: An abnormality of the cornea characterized by the deposition of calcium in a band across the central cornea, leading to decreased vision, foreign body sensation, and ocular irritation
diminution de la profondeur des orbites: Reduced depth of the orbits associated with prominent-appearing ocular globes
fente du nerf optique qui s'étend vers l: A cleft of the optic nerve that extends inferiorly
défaut de développement caractérisé par: A developmental defect characterized by a cleft of some portion of the eye or ocular adnexa
ptosis initial bilatéral suivi d'une lim: Initial bilateral ptosis followed by limitation of eye movements in all directions and slowing of saccades
coloration bleutée anormale de la scléro: An abnormal bluish coloration of the sclera
anomalie de la chambre antérieure, qui e: Abnormality of the anterior chamber, which is the space in the eye that is behind the cornea and in front of the iris
réduction de la profondeur de la chambre: Reduced depth of the anterior chamber, i.e., the anteroposterior distance between the cornea and the iris is decreased
l'ophtalmoplégie est une paralysie ou un: Ophthalmoplegia is a paralysis or weakness of one or more of the muscles that control eye movement
anomalie de l'emplacement ou de la forme: An anomaly in the placement or shape of the hairline (trichion) on the forehead, that is, the border between skin on the forehead that has head hair and that does not
une anomalie du pharynx, c'est-à-dire de: An anomaly of the pharynx, i.e., of the tubular structure extending from the base of the skull superiorly to the esophageal inlet inferiorly
une distance interpupillaire inférieure: Interpupillary distance less than 2 SD below the mean (alternatively, the appearance of an decreased interpupillary distance or closely spaced eyes)
paralysie d'un ou de plusieurs muscles e: Paralysis of one or more extraocular muscles that are responsible for eye movements
une zone de dépression visuelle située a: An area of depressed vision located at the point of fixation and that interferes with central vision
la paralysie supranucléaire du regard es: A supranuclear gaze palsy is an inability to look in a particular direction as a result of cerebral impairment. There is a loss of the voluntary aspect of eye movements, but, as the brainstem is still intact, all the reflex conjugate eye movements are normal
anomalie de la région située autour de l: An abnormality of the region situated around the orbit of the eye
terme non spécifique désignant la dégéné: A nonspecific term denoting degeneration of the retinal pigment epithelium and/or retinal photoreceptor cells of the macula lutea
toute anomalie structurelle de la choroï: Any structural abnormality of the choroid
sensibilité excessive à la lumière avec: Excessive sensitivity to light with the sensation of discomfort or pain in the eyes due to exposure to bright light
an abnormality of the nasolacrimal drain: An abnormality of the nasolacrimal drainage system, which serves as a conduit for tear flow from the external eye to the nasal cavity
an abnormality of eye movement character: An abnormality of eye movement characterized by impaired smooth-pursuit eye movements
blindness is the condition of lacking vi: Blindness is the condition of lacking visual perception defined as visual perception below 3/60 and/or a visual field of no greater than 10 degress in radius around central fixation
reduced ability to turn the eyes inward: Reduced ability to turn the eyes inward in order to focus on a nearby object
an abnormal inversion (turning inward) o: An abnormal inversion (turning inward) of the eyelid (usually the lower) towards the globe. Entropion is usually acquired as a result of involutional or cicatricial processes but may occasionally be congenital
lack of sharpness of vision resulting in: Lack of sharpness of vision resulting in the inability to see fine detail
a vertical gaze palsy with inability to: A vertical gaze palsy with inability to direct the gaze of the eyes downwards
a short discontinuity of the margin of t: A short discontinuity of the margin of the lower or upper eyelid
a posterior embryotoxon is the presence: A posterior embryotoxon is the presence of a prominent and anteriorly displaced line of Schwalbe
the presence of an increased number of t: The presence of an increased number of twists and turns of the retinal artery
abnormally decreased lacrimation, that i: Abnormally decreased lacrimation, that is, reduced ability to produce tears
an impaired ability of the eye to move i: An impaired ability of the eye to move in the outward direction (towards the side of the head)
a markedly blue coloration of the iris: A markedly blue coloration of the iris
a short discontinuity of the margin of t: A short discontinuity of the margin of the upper eyelid
distance between medial and lateral cant: Distance between medial and lateral canthi is more than two standard deviations above the mean for age (objective); or, apparently increased length of the palpebral fissures
rhythmic, involuntary oscillations of on: Rhythmic, involuntary oscillations of one or both eyes related to abnormality in fixation, conjugate gaze, or vestibular mechanisms
nystagmus made apparent by looking to th: Nystagmus made apparent by looking to the right or to the left
the controller signal for saccadic eye m: The controller signal for saccadic eye movements has two components: the pulse that moves the eye rapidly from one point to the next, and the step that holds the eye in the new position. When both the pulse and the step are not the correct size, a dysmetric refixation eye movement results
difficulty with discriminating red and g: Difficulty with discriminating red and green hues
a focal dystonia that affects the muscle: A focal dystonia that affects the muscles of the eyelids and brow, associated with involuntary recurrent spasm of both eyelids
reduced visual acuity that is uncorrecta: Reduced visual acuity that is uncorrectable by lenses in the absence of detectable anatomic defects in the eye or visual pathways
a congenital anomaly in which a part or: A congenital anomaly in which a part or the whole of the cornea acquires the characteristics of sclera, resulting in clouding of the cornea
atrophy of the optic nerve. optic atroph: Atrophy of the optic nerve. Optic atrophy results from the death of the retinal ganglion cell axons that comprise the optic nerve and manifesting as a pale optic nerve on fundoscopy
an anomaly of visually evoked potentials: An anomaly of visually evoked potentials (VEP), which are electrical potentials, initiated by brief visual stimuli, which are recorded from the scalp overlying the visual cortex
diplopia is a condition in which a singl: Diplopia is a condition in which a single object is perceived as two images, it is also known as double vision
a short discontinuity of the margin of t: A short discontinuity of the margin of the lower eyelid
descreased amplitude of eletrical respon: Descreased amplitude of eletrical response upon electroretinography
an outward turning (eversion) or rotatio: An outward turning (eversion) or rotation of the eyelid margin
ocular motor apraxia is a deficiency in: Ocular motor apraxia is a deficiency in voluntary, horizontal, lateral, fast eye movements (saccades) with retention of slow pursuit movements. The inability to follow objects visually is often compensated by head movements. There may be decreased smooth pursuit, and cancellation of the vestibulo-ocular reflex
a form of anterior segment dysgenesis in: A form of anterior segment dysgenesis in which abnormal cleavage of the anterior chamber occurs. Peters anomaly is characterized by central, paracentral, or complete corneal opacity
a creamy appearance of the retinal blood: A creamy appearance of the retinal blood vessels that occurs when the concentration of lipids in the blood are extremely increased, with pale pink to milky white retinal vessels and altered pale reflexes from choroidal vasculature
inability to see well at night or in poo: Inability to see well at night or in poor light
meeting of the medial eyebrows in the mi: Meeting of the medial eyebrows in the midline
nystagmus consisting of horizontal to-an: Nystagmus consisting of horizontal to-and-fro eye movements
atrophy of the eyeball with blindness an: Atrophy of the eyeball with blindness and decreased intraocular pressure due to end-stage intraocular disease
a developmental anomaly characterized by: A developmental anomaly characterized by a reduced number of teeth, whereby up to 6 teeth are missing
caries is a multifactorial bacterial inf: Caries is a multifactorial bacterial infection affecting the structure of the tooth. This term has been used to describe the presence of more than expected dental caries
the congenital absence of all teeth: The congenital absence of all teeth
increased size of the maxillary central: Increased size of the maxillary central secondary incisor tooth
an abnormality of the incisor tooth: An abnormality of the Incisor tooth
a developmental anomaly characterized by: A developmental anomaly characterized by a reduced number of teeth, whereby more than 6 teeth are missing
overlapping teeth within an alveolar rid: Overlapping teeth within an alveolar ridge
increased volume of dental pulp of perma: Increased volume of dental pulp of permanent molar
delayed tooth eruption affecting the pri: Delayed tooth eruption affecting the primary dentition
an abnormality of the dental enamel: An abnormality of the dental enamel
a grey discoloration of the dental ename: A grey discoloration of the dental enamel
delayed tooth eruption, which can be def: Delayed tooth eruption, which can be defined as tooth eruption more than 2 SD beyond the mean eruption age
increased spaces (diastemata) between mo: Increased spaces (diastemata) between most of the teeth in the same dental arch
dental malocclusion refers to an abnorma: Dental malocclusion refers to an abnormality of the occlusion, or alignment, of the teeth and the way the upper and lower teeth fit together, resulting in overcrowding of teeth or in abnormal bite patterns
agenesis of one or more maxillary latera: Agenesis of one or more maxillary lateral incisor, comprising the maxillary lateral primary incisor and maxillary lateral secondary incisor
decreased size of the teeth, which can b: Decreased size of the teeth, which can be defined as a mesiodistal tooth diameter (width) more than 2 SD below mean. Alternatively, an apparently decreased maximum width of tooth
abnormal alignment, positioning, or spac: Abnormal alignment, positioning, or spacing of the teeth, i.e., misaligned teeth
a type of dental dysplasia occurring in: A type of dental dysplasia occurring in dentinogenesis imperfecta in which the pulp chambers are enlarged and there is a reduced amount of coronal dentin
erupted tooth or teeth at birth: Erupted tooth or teeth at birth
delayed tooth eruption affecting the sec: Delayed tooth eruption affecting the secondary dentition
an abnormal conical form of the teeth, t: An abnormal conical form of the teeth, that is, a tooth whose sides converge or taper together incisally
increased space between two adjacent tee: Increased space between two adjacent teeth in the same dental arch
radiolucency (reflecting a reduction in: Radiolucency (reflecting a reduction in the bony substance) around the apex (the tip of the dental root)
a developmental dysplasia of the dental: A developmental dysplasia of the dental enamel
the presence of one or more embedded too: The presence of one or more embedded tooth germs which have failed to erupt
an abnormality of the nervous system: An abnormality of the nervous system
an abnormality of mental functioning inc: An abnormality of mental functioning including various affective, behavioural, cognitive and perceptual abnormalities
a condition characterized by changes of: A condition characterized by changes of personality and thought patterns often accompanied by hallucinations and delusional beliefs
a tendency or compulsion to examine obje: A tendency or compulsion to examine objects by mouth
a state of unease characterized by diffu: A state of unease characterized by diffuse motor activity or motion subject to limited control, nonproductive or disorganized behavior, and subjective distress
unstable emotional experiences and frequ: Unstable emotional experiences and frequent mood changes; emotions that are easily aroused, intense, and/or out of proportion to events and circumstances
a state of exceeding restlessness and ex: A state of exceeding restlessness and excessive motor activity associated with mental distress or a feeling of inner tension
frequent feelings of being down, miserab: Frequent feelings of being down, miserable, and/or hopeless; difficulty recovering from such moods; pessimism about the future; pervasive shame; feeling of inferior self-worth; thoughts of suicide and suicidal behavior
autism is a neurodevelopmental disorder: Autism is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by impaired social interaction and communication, and by restricted and repetitive behavior. Autism begins in childhood. It is marked by the presence of markedly abnormal or impaired development in social interaction and communication and a markedly restricted repertoire of activity and interest. Manifestations of the disorder vary greatly depending on the developmental level and chronological age of the individual (DSM-IV)
aggressive behavior can denote verbal ag: Aggressive behavior can denote verbal aggression, physical aggression against objects, physical aggression against people, and may also include aggression towards oneself
an exaggeration of emotional affects suc: An exaggeration of emotional affects such as laughing crying, or yawning beyond what the person feels
recurrent obsessions or compulsions that: Recurrent obsessions or compulsions that are severe enough to be time consuming (i.e., they take more than 1 hour a day) or cause marked distress or significant impairment (DSM-IV)
behavior characterized by an abnormal li: Behavior characterized by an abnormal limitation to few interests and activities
a loss of global cognitive ability of su: A loss of global cognitive ability of sufficient amount to interfere with normal social or occupational function. Dementia represents a loss of previously present cognitive abilities, generally in adults, and can affect memory, thinking, language, judgment, and behavior
persistent deficits in social interactio: Persistent deficits in social interaction and communication and interaction as well as a markedly restricted repertoire of activity and interest as well as repetitive patterns of behavior
a stereotypy is a repetitive, simple mov: A stereotypy is a repetitive, simple movement that can be voluntarily suppressed. Stereotypies are typically simple back-and-forth movements such as waving of flapping the hands or arms, and they do not involve complex sequences or movement fragments. Movement is often but not always rhythmic and may involve fingers, wrists, or more proximal portions of the upper extremity. The lower extremity is not typically involved. Stereotypies are more commonly bilateral than unilateral
a lack of restraint manifested in severa: A lack of restraint manifested in several ways, including disregard for social conventions, impulsivity, and poor risk assessment
difficulty in social interactions relate: Difficulty in social interactions related to an impairment of characteristics such as eye contact, smiling, appropriate facial expressions, and body postures and characterized by difficulty in forming peer relationships and forming friendships
reduced attention span characterized by: Reduced attention span characterized by distractibility and impulsivity but not necessarily satisfying the diagnostic criteria for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder
perceptions in a conscious and awake sta: Perceptions in a conscious and awake state in the absence of external stimuli which have qualities of real perception, in that they are vivid, substantial, and located in external objective space
intense feelings of nervousness, tensene: Intense feelings of nervousness, tenseness, or panic, often in reaction to interpersonal stresses; worry about the negative effects of past unpleasant experiences and future negative possibilities; feeling fearful, apprehensive, or threatened by uncertainty; fears of falling apart or losing control
recurrent attacks of severe anxiety, who: Recurrent attacks of severe anxiety, whose occurence is not restricted to any particular situation or set of circumstances and is therefore unpredictable
primitive reflexes traditionally held to: Primitive reflexes traditionally held to be a sign of disorders that affect the frontal lobes
the feeling of frustration can be define: The feeling of frustration can be defined as an emotional reaction that occurs if a desired goal is not achieved. Frustration intolerance is defined as an age-inappropriate response to frustration characterized by crying or temper tantrums (in children) or aggressive or other undesirable behaviors
a reduction in goal-directed behavior, t: A reduction in goal-directed behavior, that is, motivation, the determinant of behavior and adaptation that allows individuals to get started, be energized to perform a sustained and directed action
a belief that is pathological and is hel: A belief that is pathological and is held despite evidence to the contrary
a degree of language development that is: A degree of language development that is significantly below the norm for a child of a specified age
an abnormal shift in patterns of thinkin: An abnormal shift in patterns of thinking, acting, or feeling
hyperactivity is a state of constantly b: Hyperactivity is a state of constantly being unusually or abnormally active, including in situations in which it is not appropriate
a type of anxiety disorder characterized: A type of anxiety disorder characterized by avoidance of public places, especially where crowds gather
reduced ability to use nonverbal behavio: Reduced ability to use nonverbal behavior for communication, such as eye-to-eye gaze, facial expression, body posture, and gestures
a structural abnormality of the peripher: A structural abnormality of the peripheral nervous system, which is composed of the nerves that lead to or branch off from the central nervous system. This includes the cranial nerves (olfactory and optic nerves are technically part of the central nervous system)
a reduction in the speed at which electr: A reduction in the speed at which electrical signals propagate along the axon of a neuron
peripheral neuropathy affecting the sens: Peripheral neuropathy affecting the sensory nerves
any abnormality of the thorax (the regio: Any abnormality of the thorax (the region of the body formed by the sternum, the thoracic vertebrae and the ribs)
an anomaly of the sternum, also known as: An anomaly of the sternum, also known as the breastbone
a defect of the chest wall characterized: A defect of the chest wall characterized by a depression of the sternum, giving the chest ("pectus") a caved-in ("excavatum") appearance
a deformity of the chest caused by overg: A deformity of the chest caused by overgrowth of the ribs and characterized by protrusion of the sternum
abnormal development of large mammary gl: Abnormal development of large mammary glands in males resulting in breast enlargement
reduced width of the chest from side to: Reduced width of the chest from side to side, associated with a reduced distance from the sternal notch to the tip of the shoulder
any abnormality of the diaphragm, the sh: Any abnormality of the diaphragm, the sheet of skeletal muscle that separates the thoracic cavity from the abdominal cavity
the presence of a hernia of the diaphrag: The presence of a hernia of the diaphragm present at birth
abnormality of the thymus, an organ loca: Abnormality of the thymus, an organ located in the upper anterior portion of the chest cavity just behind the sternum and whose main function is to provide an environment for T lymphocyte maturation
any abnormality of the scapula, also kno: Any abnormality of the scapula, also known as the shoulder blade
the presence of calculi (stones) in the: The presence of calculi (stones) in the kidneys
the presence of blood in the urine. hema: The presence of blood in the urine. Hematuria may be gross hematuria (visible to the naked eye) or microscopic hematuria (detected by dipstick or microscopic examination of the urine)
the presence of uric acid-containing cal: The presence of uric acid-containing calculi (stones) in the kidneys
a type of glomerulonephritis characteriz: A type of glomerulonephritis characterized by diffuse mesangial cell proliferation and the thickening of capillary walls due to subendothelial extension of the mesangium. The term membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis is often employed to denote a general pattern of glomerular injury seen in a variety of disease processes that share a common pathogenetic mechanism, rather than to describe a single disease entity
the presence of immunoglobulin a deposit: The presence of immunoglobulin A deposits in the glomerulus
an abnormality of the urethra, i.e., of: An abnormality of the urethra, i.e., of the tube which connects the urinary bladder to the outside of the body
obstruction of the flow of urine through: Obstruction of the flow of urine through the urethra
reduced count of spermatozoa in the seme: Reduced count of spermatozoa in the semen, defined as a sperm count below 20 million per milliliter semen
abnormal fat accumulation in the kidneys: Abnormal fat accumulation in the kidneys
cysts of the cortex of the kidney: Cysts of the cortex of the kidney
the presence of xanthine-containing calc: The presence of xanthine-containing calculi (stones) in the kidneys
lack of the ability to control the urina: Lack of the ability to control the urinary bladder leading to involuntary urination at an age where control of the bladder should already be possible
a severe form of hypospadias in which th: A severe form of hypospadias in which the urethral opening is located at the junction of the penis and scrotum
congenital absence of the normal opening: Congenital absence of the normal opening of a structure of the urinary tract
utérus bicorne hypoplasique: An anomaly of the adnexa, uterus, and vagina (in female) or seminal tract and prostate (in male)
an abnormality of the tricarboxylic acid: An abnormality of the tricarboxylic acid cycle
difficulty in looking at another person: Difficulty in looking at another person in the eye
the presence of chronic increased pressu: The presence of chronic increased pressure in the systemic arterial system
passing the age when puberty normally oc: Passing the age when puberty normally occurs with no physical or hormonal signs of the onset of puberty
insufficient production of growth hormon: Insufficient production of growth hormone, which is produced by the anterior pituitary gland. Growth hormone is a major participant in control of growth and metabolism
an increased concentration of insulin co: An increased concentration of insulin combined with a decreased concentration of glucose in the blood
the onset of secondary sexual characteri: The onset of secondary sexual characteristics before a normal age. Although it is difficult to define normal age ranges because of the marked variation with which puberty begins in normal children, precocious puberty can be defined as the onset of puberty before the age of 8 years in girls or 9 years in boys
an abnormality of the parathyroid gland: An abnormality of the parathyroid gland
a condition caused by a deficiency of pa: A condition caused by a deficiency of parathyroid hormone characterized by hypocalcemia and hyperphosphatemia
a condition of reduced function of the a: A condition of reduced function of the anterior pituitary gland characterized by decreased secretion of one or more of the pituitary hormones growth hormone, thyroid-stimulating hormone, adrenocorticotropic hormone, prolactin, luteinizing hormone, and follicle-stimulating hormone
a type of diabetes mellitus related not: A type of diabetes mellitus related not to lack of insulin but rather to lack of response to insulin on the part of the target tissues of insulin such as muscle, fat, and liver cells. This type of diabetes is typically associated with increases both in blood glucose concentrations as will as in fasting and postprandial serum insulin levels
a type of hypothyroidism that results fr: A type of hypothyroidism that results from a defect in the thyroid gland
developmental hypoplasia of the adrenal: Developmental hypoplasia of the adrenal glands
an abnormality of thyroid physiology cha: An abnormality of thyroid physiology characterized by excessive secretion of the thyroid hormones thyroxine (i.e., T4) and/or 3,3',5-triiodo-L-thyronine zwitterion (i.e., triiodothyronine or T3)
production anté-hypophysaire excessive d: Overproduction of gonadotropins (FSH, LH) by the anterior pituitary gland
a type of reduced stature with normal pr: A type of reduced stature with normal proportions related to dysfunction of the pituitary gland related to either an isolated defect in the secretion of growth hormone or to panhypopituitarism, i.e., a deficit of all the anterior pituitary hormones
adrenogenital syndrome is also known as: Adrenogenital syndrome is also known as congenital adrenal hyperplasia, which results from disorders of steroid hormone production in the adrenal glands leading to a deficiency of cortisol. The pituitary gland reacts by increased secretion of corticotropin, which in turn causes the adrenal glands to overproduce certain intermediary hormones which have testosterone-like effects
