compression of the heart caused by rigid: Compression of the heart caused by rigid, thickened, or fused pericardial membranes
enlargement of the chamber of the right: Enlargement of the chamber of the right ventricle
refers to the specific combination of de: Refers to the specific combination of defects with a severely dysplastic pulmonary valve and massively dilated branch pulmonary arteries
an abnormality of the t wave on the elec: An abnormality of the T wave on the electrocardiogram, which mainly represents the repolarization of the ventricles
mitral annular calcification (mac) resul: Mitral annular calcification (MAC) results from progressive calcium deposition along and beneath the mitral valve annulus
the right pulmonary artery originates fr: The right pulmonary artery originates from the ascending aorta in the presence of a pulmonary valve and main pulmonary artery
the dividing wall between left and right: The dividing wall between left and right sides of the heart, thickens and bulges into the left ventricle
abnormal narrowing of the coronary arter: Abnormal narrowing of the coronary artery
abnormal calcification of a cardiac valv: Abnormal calcification of a cardiac valve
any defect in the valve connecting the h: Any defect in the valve connecting the heart and the pulmonary artery
an impairment of the electrical continui: An impairment of the electrical continuity between the atria and ventricles
narrowing or constriction of the aorta l: Narrowing or constriction of the aorta localized proximal to the ductus arteriosus, i.e., to the preductal region of aortic arch
a type of cardiomyopathy characterized p: A type of cardiomyopathy characterized pathologically by hamartomatous lesions of cardiac Purkinje cells
a ventircular escape rhythm occurs whene: A ventircular escape rhythm occurs whenever higher-lever pacemakers in AV junction or sinus node fail to control ventricular activation. Escape rate is usually 20-40 bpm, often associated with broad QRS complexes are broad (at least 120 ms)
hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with an symm: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with an symmetrical and concentric pattern of hypertrophy
total anomalous pulmonary venous return: Total anomalous pulmonary venous return refers to a congenital malformation in which all four pulmonary veins do not connect normally to the left atrium, but instead drain abnormally to the right atrium
inability of the left ventricle to perfo: Inability of the left ventricle to perform its normal physiologic function. Failure is either due to an inability to contract the left ventricle or the inability to relax completely and fill with blood during diastole
a congenital malformation of the pulmona: A congenital malformation of the pulmonary valve characterized by leaflet deformation
reduced time for the pr interval (beginn: Reduced time for the PR interval (beginning of the P wave to the beginning of the QRS complex). In adults, normal values are 120 to 200 ms long
an abnormal increase in magnitude of the: An abnormal increase in magnitude of the pressure in the right atrium
a type of third degree heart block in wh: A type of third degree heart block in which the escape rhythm arises at a relatively low part of the conduction system (below the atrioventricular node), which produces a wide QRS complex
conduction block in the posterior divisi: Conduction block in the posterior division of the left bundle branch of the bundle of His
subvalvular stenosis is caused by a diap: Subvalvular stenosis is caused by a diaphragm-like membrane. The stenosis is clinically manifested like any other form of aortic stenosis but is often associated with some aortic insufficiency
a congenital malformation of the aortic: A congenital malformation of the aortic valve characterized by leaflet deformation
arteriosclerosis occurring at an age tha: Arteriosclerosis occurring at an age that is younger than usual
a type of third degree heart block in wh: A type of third degree heart block in which the escape rhythm arises at the atrioventricular node, which produces a narrow QRS complex
failure of the tricuspid valve to close: Failure of the tricuspid valve to close sufficiently upon contraction of the right ventricle, causing blood to regurgitate (flow backward) into the right atrium
reduction of the diameter of the coronar: Reduction of the diameter of the coronary arteries as the result of an accumulation of atheromatous plaques within the walls of the coronary arteries before age of 45
the presence of a bicuspid pulmonary val: The presence of a bicuspid pulmonary valve
an abnormal dilatation of lymph vessels: An abnormal dilatation of lymph vessels in the pericardium
a longer than normal interval (corrected: A longer than normal interval (corrected for heart rate) between the Q and T waves in the heart's cycle. Prolonged QTc can cause premature action potentials during late phase depolarizations thereby leading to ventricular arrhythmias and ventricular fibrillations
a reduced ejection fraction and an enlar: A reduced ejection fraction and an enlarged left ventricle chamber, the latter by an increased resistance to filling with increased filling pressures. Systolic dysfunction is clinically associated with left ventricular failure in the presence of marked cardiomegaly
abnormally flat shape of the heads of th: Abnormally flat shape of the heads of the metatarsal bones
a sensation of stiffness in the joints t: A sensation of stiffness in the joints that occurs following waking up in the morning
interphalangeal joint stiffness is a per: Interphalangeal joint stiffness is a perceived sensation of tightness in the interphalangeal joints when attempting to move them after a period of inactivity
a recurrent infection of the gi tract wi: A recurrent infection of the GI tract with helicobacter pylori, a gram-negative, microaerophilic bacterium usually found in the stomach
the occurrence of the full-thickness tea: The occurrence of the full-thickness tear (perforation) of the wall of the esophagus
cyst-like space not lined by epithelium: Cyst-like space not lined by epithelium and contained within the pancreas. Pancreatic pseudocysts are often associated with pancreatitis
watery voluminous diarrhea resulting fro: Watery voluminous diarrhea resulting from an imbalance between ion and water secretion and absorption
the presence of cyst of the intrahepatic: The presence of cyst of the intrahepatic bile duct
leukoplakia is a precancerous dermatosis: Leukoplakia is a precancerous dermatosis of mucous membranes analogous Leukoplakia is basically a chronic inflammatory hypertrophy in which anaplasia and malignant dyskeratosis may develop and subsequently advance to an invasive squamous cell cancer. The clinical diagnosis of primary anal leukoplakia is indicated by single or multiple slightly raised,irregular, marginated, grayish-white keratinized' patches in the anal canal. Tissue biopsy is necessary for confirmation
the presence of abnormal calcium deposit: The presence of abnormal calcium deposition lesions in the pancreas
blockage or impairment of the normal flo: Blockage or impairment of the normal flow of the contents of the intestine towards the anal canal
increased susceptibility to giardia lamb: Increased susceptibility to Giardia lamblia infection of the intestine, as manifested by a medical history of multiple episodes of Giardia lamblia intestinal infection
an abnormal reduction in the ability to: An abnormal reduction in the ability to masticate (chew), i.e., in the ability to crush and ground food in preparation for swallowing
the presence of a fistula (abnormal tunn: The presence of a fistula (abnormal tunnel) between the anal canal and the perineum
absence of gastric intrinsic factor, whi: Absence of gastric intrinsic factor, which is normally produced by the parietal cells of the stomach, and is required for the absorption of vitamin B12
the presence of multiple diverticula of: The presence of multiple diverticula of the intestine
a collection of pus in the area of the r: A collection of pus in the area of the rectum
accumulation of cell free, noninflammato: Accumulation of cell free, noninflammatony fluid within the wall of the intestinal tract producing uniform thickening of the mucosal folds
presence of multiple adenomatous polyps: Presence of multiple adenomatous polyps in the colon
the presence of developmental dysplasia: The presence of developmental dysplasia of the pancreas
the presence of a hypoplastic gallbladde: The presence of a hypoplastic gallbladder
a developmental defect resulting in abno: A developmental defect resulting in abnormal closure, or atresia of the tubular structure of the jejunum
a form of chronic pancreatitis that is c: A form of chronic pancreatitis that is characterized by calcification
the presence of degenerative changes of: The presence of degenerative changes of the liver
a congenital defect characterized by the: A congenital defect characterized by the lack of ganglion cells in the entire intestine, i.e., the aganglionic segment comprises the entire large and small bowel
atresia in the extrahepatic bile duct: Atresia in the extrahepatic bile duct
failure to form of portions of the abdom: Failure to form of portions of the abdominal musculature
a type of gastritis characterized by exc: A type of gastritis characterized by excessive proliferation of the gastric mucosa and diffuse thickening of the gastric mucosal folds
atresia in the intrahepatic bile duct: Atresia in the intrahepatic bile duct
aplasia (congenital absence) of the pect: Aplasia (congenital absence) of the pectoralis minor
Aplasia (congenital absence) of the pectoralis minor on only one side of the chest
the presence of inflammation of the gast: The presence of inflammation of the gastric mucous membrane
an abnormality of the jejunum, i.e., of: An abnormality of the jejunum, i.e., of the middle section of the small intestine
a discrete abnormal tissue mass that pro: A discrete abnormal tissue mass that protrudes into the lumen of the intestine and is attached to the intestinal wall either by a stalk, pedunculus, or a broad base
a pregnancy that ends at a stage in whic: A pregnancy that ends at a stage in which the fetus is incapable of surviving on its own, defined as the spontaneous loss of a fetus before the 20th week of pregnancy
exaggerated bulkiness of the crease or f: Exaggerated bulkiness of the crease or fold of skin running from the lateral margin of the nose, where nasal base meets the skin of the face, to a point just lateral to the corner of the mouth (cheilion, or commissure)
lack of the cartilage of the nasal septu: Lack of the cartilage of the nasal septum
posterior positioning of the nasal root: Posterior positioning of the nasal root in relation to the overall facial profile for age
a separation (dissection) of the layers: A separation (dissection) of the layers of an artery
pseudocoarctation is a congenital anomal: Pseudocoarctation is a congenital anomaly of kinking, or buckling, of the aorta without a pressure gradient across the lesion. It is characterized by elongation and kinking of the aorta at the level of the ligamentum arteriosum
peripheral arterial stenosis with onset: Peripheral arterial stenosis with onset before the age of 50 years
a rare congenital vascular anomaly that: A rare congenital vascular anomaly that results when the left superior cardinal vein caudal to the innominate vein fails to regress
abnormal tortuous (i.e., twisted) form o: Abnormal tortuous (i.e., twisted) form of the carotid arteries
calcification, that is, pathological dep: Calcification, that is, pathological deposition of calcium salts in the arch of aorta
formation of a blood clot (thrombus) ins: Formation of a blood clot (thrombus) inside a cerebral vein, causing the obstruction of blood flow
the presence of a capillary hemangiomas,: The presence of a capillary hemangiomas, which are hemangiomas with small endothelial spaces
a type of vasculitis (inflammation of bl: A type of vasculitis (inflammation of blood vessel walls) affecting large arteries such as the aorta and branches of the aorta
formation of excess fibrous connective t: Formation of excess fibrous connective tissue in the tunica intima (innermost layer) of arteries in the pulmonary circulation
an arterial lesion that is characterized: An arterial lesion that is characterized by either intimal fibroplasia, with neointimal lesions of cells and matrix deposition, or medial fibroplasia, in which there is loss of smooth muscle cells and increased deposition of collagen and proteoglycans in the medial layer
inflammation of the blood vessels within: Inflammation of the blood vessels within the brain
a type of craniofacial dysostosis associ: A type of craniofacial dysostosis associated with abnormalities of the external ears, mirognathia, macrostomia, coloboma of the lower eyelid, and cleft palate. This is a bundled term that is left in the HPO now for convenience with legacy annotations but should not be used for new annotations
unilateral overgrowth of facial tissues,: Unilateral overgrowth of facial tissues, including muscles, bones and skin
an abnormality of the gestures or moveme: An abnormality of the gestures or movements executed with the facial muscles with which emotions such as fear, joy, sadness, surprise, and disgust can be expressed
a pattern of hair growth in which there: A pattern of hair growth in which there is hair exnteding from the temples to the lateral eyebrows
a degree of wrinkling of the facial skin: A degree of wrinkling of the facial skin that is more than expected for the age of the individual, leading to a prematurely aged appearance
recurrent partial dislocations of the ma: Recurrent partial dislocations of the mandible
decreased density/number and/or decrease: Decreased density/number and/or decreased diameter of lateral eyebrow hairs
an abnormality of the complement system: An abnormality of the complement system
abnormal bladder function (increased urg: Abnormal bladder function (increased urge or frequency of urination or urge incontinence) resulting from abnormal functioning of the autonomic nervous system
any structural abnormality of the caroti: Any structural abnormality of the carotid arteries, including the common carotid artery and its' arterial branches
an abnormality of the structure of the v: An abnormality of the structure of the veins that return deoxygenated blood from the body into the heart, i.e., the superior vena cava and the inferior vena cava
inspiratory stridor is a high pitched so: Inspiratory stridor is a high pitched sound upon inspiration that is generally related to laryngeal abnormalities
a primary immune deficiency that is char: A primary immune deficiency that is characterized by defects or deficiencies of T-lymphocytes that causes specific susceptibility to intracellular micro-organisms
increased susceptibility to herpesvirus,: Increased susceptibility to herpesvirus, as manifested by recurrent episodes of herpesvirus
complete inability of t cells to perform: Complete inability of T cells to perform their functions in cell-mediated immunity
a reduced level of the complement compon: A reduced level of the complement component Factor I in circulation
reduced functionality of the process in: Reduced functionality of the process in which a precursor cell type acquires the specialized features of a B cell. A B cell is a lymphocyte of B lineage with the phenotype CD19-positive and capable of B cell mediated immunity
absence of the thymus. this feature may: Absence of the thymus. This feature may be appreciated by the lack of a thymic shadow upon radiographic examination
increased susceptibility to chicken pox,: Increased susceptibility to chicken pox, as manifested by recurrent episodes of chicken pox
a general term referring to a defect in: A general term referring to a defect in immunity resulting from impaired antibody production
a severe form of b lymphocytopenia in wh: A severe form of B lymphocytopenia in which the count of B cells is very low or absent
increased susceptibility to streptococcu: Increased susceptibility to streptococcus pneumoniae infections as manifested by a history of recurrent infections by streptococcus pneumoniae
an abnormality of the humoral immune sys: An abnormality of the humoral immune system, which comprises antibodies produced by B cells as well as the complement system
a reduced level of the complement compon: A reduced level of the complement component Factor H in circulation
an abnormality of the physiological func: An abnormality of the physiological functioning of B cells
an immunodeficiency characterized by def: An immunodeficiency characterized by defective cell-mediated immunity or humoral immunity
increased susceptibility to haemophilus: Increased susceptibility to Haemophilus influenzae infections as manifested by recurrent episodes of infection by Haemophilus influenzae
recurrent infections by neisseria mening: Recurrent infections by Neisseria meningitidis (one of the most common causes of bacterial meningitis), which is also known as meningococcus
a reduced ability of a b cell to become: A reduced ability of a B cell to become activated, i.e., the change in morphology and behavior of
increased susceptibility to protozoan in: Increased susceptibility to protozoan infections, as manifested by recurrent episodes of protozoan infection
a group of phenotypically heterogeneous: A group of phenotypically heterogeneous genetic disorders characterized by profound deficiencies of T- and B-cell function, which predispose the patients to both infectious and noninfectious complications
an abnormal reduction in the components: An abnormal reduction in the components of the alternative complement pathway, such as the C3 protein or its cleavage products
increased susceptibility to opportunisti: Increased susceptibility to opportunistic infections, as manifested by recurrent episodes of infection by opportunistic agents, i.e., by microorganisms that do not usually cause disease in a healthy host, but are able to infect a host with a compromised immune system
increased susceptibility to coronavirus: Increased susceptibility to coronavirus 229e, as manifested by recurrent episodes of coronavirus 229e
an abnormal reduction of the cell motili: An abnormal reduction of the cell motility of neutrophils
an increased susceptibility to candida i: An increased susceptibility to candida infections, as manifested by a history of recurrent episodes of candida infections
an abnormally low count of t cells: An abnormally low count of T cells
an abnormal increase from the normal cou: An abnormal increase from the normal count of B cells
increased susceptibility to bacterial in: Increased susceptibility to bacterial infections of the skin, as manifested by recurrent episodes of infectious dermatitis
a decreased proportion of circulating cd: A decreased proportion of circulating CD4-positive helper T cells relative to total T cell count
persistent overgrowth of candida albican: Persistent overgrowth of Candida albicans in the gastrointestinal tract
an abnormally increased level of circula: An abnormally increased level of circulationg alpha-globulin. Alpha globulins are a group of serum proteins defined by their mobility on serum electrophoresis. The alpha1-protein fraction is comprised of alpha1-antitrypsin, thyroid-binding globulin, and transcortin. Ceruloplasmin, alpha2-macroglobulin, and haptoglobin contribute to the alpha2-protein band. The alpha2 component is increased as an acute-phase reactant
a decreased proportion of circulating cd: A decreased proportion of circulating CD8-positive, alpha-beta T cells relative to total number of T cells
a reduced level of the complement compon: A reduced level of the complement component factor B in circulation
decreased or impaired activation of t ce: Decreased or impaired activation of T cells in response to a mitogen, cytokine, chemokine, cellular ligand, or an antigen for which it is specific
increased susceptibility to infection by: Increased susceptibility to infection by gram-negative bacteria, as manifested by a medical history of repeated or frequent infections by these agents
a reduced level of the complement compon: A reduced level of the complement component C3 in circulation
lack of detectible cd8-positive t cells: Lack of detectible CD8-positive T cells
an abnormality of the functioning of any: An abnormality of the functioning of any aspect of the alternative complement pathway
absence of specific immunoglobulins dire: Absence of specific immunoglobulins directed against a specific antigen or microorganism
an increased susceptibility to infection: An increased susceptibility to infections involving both the paranasal sinuses and the lungs, as manifested by a history of recurrent sinopulmonary infections
increased susceptibility to systemic pyo: Increased susceptibility to systemic pyogenic infections, as manifested by recurrent episodes of systemic pyogenic infections
recurrent infections by bacteria of the: Recurrent infections by bacteria of the genus Neisseria, including N. meningitidis (one of the most common causes of bacterial meningitis)
at birth, newborns are endowed with mate: At birth, newborns are endowed with maternal antibodies. IgG production normally begins at the age of two months. A delay in recovery from this physiological hypogammaglobulinemia between the 3rd and the 6th month of life, and of recovery period between 18 and 36 months defines transient newborn hypogammaglobulinemia
abnormally reduced ability of t cells to: Abnormally reduced ability of T cells to perform their functions in cell-mediated immunity
recurrent infections at an early age wit: Recurrent infections at an early age with improvement in later childhood
an increased density in the cranial sutu: An increased density in the cranial sutures following obliteration
the presence of a coronal suture (the cr: The presence of a coronal suture (the cranial suture that separates the frontal and parietal bones) that is not ossified but rather wide open at an age when it is normally closed
abnormally flat (obtuse) angle of the ma: Abnormally flat (obtuse) angle of the mandible. The angle of the mandibular, located at the junction between the body and the ramus of the mandible, is normally close to being a right angle. This terms describes an abnormal increase of this angle such that the mandible appears flatter than normal
an increase in bone density affecting th: An increase in bone density affecting the calvaria (roof of the skull)
aplasia or hypoplasia of the paranasal s: Aplasia or hypoplasia of the paranasal sinuses
normally, the posterior and lateral font: Normally, the posterior and lateral fontanelles are obliterated by about six months after birth, the anterior fontanelle closes by about the middle of the second year. This term refers to the situation in which the fontanelles close at an inappropriately early time point
the presence of calcium deposition in th: The presence of calcium deposition in the falx cerebri
abnormally increased density of craniofa: Abnormally increased density of craniofacial bone tissue
excessive growth (overgrowth) of the fac: Excessive growth (overgrowth) of the facial bones, that is of the facial skeleton
reduced convexity of the occiput (poster: Reduced convexity of the occiput (posterior part of skull)
bony fusion of malleus, incus, and stape: Bony fusion of malleus, incus, and stapes
abnormal reduction in ossification of th: Abnormal reduction in ossification of the calvaria (roof of the skull consisting of the frontal bone, parietal bones, temporal bones, and occipital bone)
the presence of a sagittal suture (the c: The presence of a sagittal suture (the cranial suture that separates the left and right parietal bones) that is not ossified but rather wide open at an age when it is normally closed
progressively increasing bone density of: Progressively increasing bone density of the skull base without significant changes in bony contour
an abnormally decreased level of immunog: An abnormally decreased level of immunoglobulin E (IgE) in blood
a deviation in any aspect of the alterna: A deviation in any aspect of the alternative complement pathway
microcephaly (hp:0000252) with onset in: Microcephaly (HP:0000252) with onset in the postnatal period, that is, the head circumference is in the normal range at birth but falls behind normal values later in development
a fontanelle that is small for age: A fontanelle that is small for age
vertical bony ridge positioned in the mi: Vertical bony ridge positioned in the midline of the forehead
the postnatal development of an abnormal: The postnatal development of an abnormally large skull (macrocephaly)
the frontal suture divides the two halve: The frontal suture divides the two halves of the frontal bone in infants and usually fuses by the age of six years. The suture runs from the bregma (the point on the skull at which the coronal suture is intersected perpendicularly by the sagittal suture) to the nasion or nasal root. This term applies if the suture is widely patent from bregma to nasal root
a myeloproliferative disorder characteri: A myeloproliferative disorder characterized by increased proliferation of the granulocytic cell line without the loss of their capacity to differentiate
normal adult hemoglobin is composed of t: Normal adult hemoglobin is composed of two chains each of alpha and beta globin. Hb Barts (Hemoglobin Barts) is a tetramer with four gamma globin chains, and is essentially pathognomonic for one or another form of alpha thalassemia. Hb Barts has an extremely high affinity for oxygen, resulting in almost no oxygen delivery to the tissues
presence of a monoclonal immunoglobulin: Presence of a monoclonal immunoglobulin M protein in the serum
a transient reduction in the number of e: A transient reduction in the number of erythroblasts in the circulation
anemia characterized by abnormal intrace: Anemia characterized by abnormal intracellular inclusions, composed of denatured hemoglobin, found on the membrane of red blood cells
a reduction in the ability of neutrophil: A reduction in the ability of neutrophils to kill the gram-positive bacteria, staphylococcus, which is commonly known as staph
increased megakaryocyte number, i.e., of: Increased megakaryocyte number, i.e., of platelet precursor cells, present in the bone marrow
a type of t-cell lymphoma in which cance: A type of T-cell lymphoma in which cancerous T-cells may present in the blood (leukemia), lymph nodes (lymphoma), skin or in multiple areas
larger than normal size of erythrocytes: Larger than normal size of erythrocytes
a chronic form of disseminated intravasc: A chronic form of disseminated intravascular coagulation in which a persistent weak or intermittent activating stimulus is present and destruction and production of coagulation factors and platelets are balanced
disseminated intravascular coagulation i: Disseminated intravascular coagulation is characterized by the widespread activation of coagulation, which results in the intravascular formation of fibrin and ultimately thrombotic occlusion of small and midsize vessels
a type of sideroblastic anemia that is a: A type of sideroblastic anemia that is alleviated by pyridoxine (vitamin B-6) treatment
a malignant lymphocytic neoplasm of b-ce: A malignant lymphocytic neoplasm of B-cell or T-cell lineage involving primarily the bone marrow and the peripheral blood. This category includes precursor or acute lymphoblastic leukemias and chronic leukemias
reduction in the amount of kininogen, wh: Reduction in the amount of kininogen, which functions as a cofactor in the contact phase of the intrinsic blood coagulation cascade
a reduced number of hematopoietic cells: A reduced number of hematopoietic cells present in the bone marrow relative to marrow fat
a type of actue leukemia with features c: A type of actue leukemia with features characteristic of both the myeloid and lymphoid lineages. These leukemias are for this reason are designated mixed-lineage, hybrid or biphenotypic acute leukemias
a unique clonal neoplastic disorder that: A unique clonal neoplastic disorder that is linked to trisomy 21, is restricted to neonatal period, and spontaneously regresses. It often has characteristics of megakaryocytic lineage and is associated with GATA1 mutations in myeloblasts
a form of hemolytic anemia that can be t: A form of hemolytic anemia that can be triggered by exertion
average platelet volume below the lower: Average platelet volume below the lower limit of the normal reference interval
a form of lymphoid leukemia or lymphoma: A form of lymphoid leukemia or lymphoma in which too many T-cell lymphoblasts are found in the blood, bone marrow, and tissues. Leukemia or lymphoma classification depends on which feature is more prominent
a form of poikilocytosis in which the ab: A form of poikilocytosis in which the abnormally shaped erythrocytes have notches that results in projections that look like horns
congenital onset of a marked decrease in: Congenital onset of a marked decrease in the number of granulocytes
an abnormal prolongation (delay) in the: An abnormal prolongation (delay) in the time required by whole blood to produce a visible clot
an abnormality of coagulation related to: An abnormality of coagulation related to a decreased concentration of vitamin K-dependent protein C. Protein C is activated to protein Ca by thrombin bound to thrombomodulin. Activated protein C degrades factors VIIIa and Va
proliferation (excess production) of hem: Proliferation (excess production) of hemopoietically active tissue or of tissue which has embryonic hemopoietic potential
a chronic lymphocytic/lymphatic/lymphobl: A chronic lymphocytic/lymphatic/lymphoblastic leukemia (CLL) is a neoplastic disease characterized by proliferation and accumulation (blood, marrow and lymphoid organs) of morphologically mature but immunologically dysfunctional lymphocytes. A CLL is always a B-cell lymphocytic leukemia as there are no reports of cases of T-cell lymphocytic leukemias
the frontal suture divides the two halve: The frontal suture divides the two halves of the frontal bone of the skull in infants and children and generally undergoes fusion by the age of six. A persistent frontal suture is referred to as a "metopic suture"
an abnormality of the zygomatic arch, al: An abnormality of the zygomatic arch, also known as the cheek bone
a slowly progressing leukemia characteri: A slowly progressing leukemia characterized by a clonal (malignant) proliferation of maturing and mature myeloid cells or mature lymphocytes. When the clonal cellular population is composed of myeloid cells, the process is called chronic myelogenous leukemia. When the clonal cellular population is composed of lymphocytes, it is classified as chronic lymphocytic leukemia, hairy cell leukemia, or T-cell large granular lymphocyte leukemia
normal hemoglobin synthesis is character: Normal hemoglobin synthesis is characterized by production of equal amounts of alpha and beta globins. This term refers to a deviation from this pattern and is the main characteristic of the various forms of thalassemia
an anomaly of the form or number of cell: An anomaly of the form or number of cells in the bone marrow
the presence of many cysts in the kidney: The presence of many cysts in the kidney
a reduction in the count of nephrons per: A reduction in the count of nephrons per kidney
a lack of differentiation between renal: A lack of differentiation between renal cortex and medulla on diagnostic imaging
reduced differentiation between renal co: Reduced differentiation between renal cortex and medulla on diagnostic imaging
high urine magnesium in the presence of: High urine magnesium in the presence of hypomagnesemia
a type of proximal renal tubulopathy cha: A type of proximal renal tubulopathy characterized by resorption defects leading to glycosuria, aminoaciduria, tubular proteinuria, renal hypophosphatemia, and urate tubular hyporeabsorption without bicarbonate loss
a progressive detrimental connective tis: A progressive detrimental connective tissue deposition (fibrosis) on the kidney parenchyma involving the tubules and interstitial tissue of the kidney
a duplication of the renal pelvis: A duplication of the renal pelvis
disruption and breaking up of the baseme: DIsruption and breaking up of the basement membrane of the tubules of the kidney
a type of carcinoma of the kidney with o: A type of carcinoma of the kidney with origin in the epithelium of the proximal convoluted renal tubule
a focal type of palmoplantar keratoderma: A focal type of palmoplantar keratoderma in which only certain areas of the palms and soles are affected
the presence of large spherical melanoso: The presence of large spherical melanosomes (1 to 6 micrometer in diameter) in the cytoplasm of melanocytes
loss of all scalp hair with congenital o: Loss of all scalp hair with congenital onset
telangiectases (small dilated blood vess: Telangiectases (small dilated blood vessels) located near the surface of the skin in a butterfly midface distribution
the giant congenital nevus is greater th: The giant congenital nevus is greater than 8 cm in size, pigmented and often hairy. A giant congenital nevus is smaller in infants and children, but it usually continues to grow with the child
large hypermelanotic macules with jagged: Large hypermelanotic macules with jagged borders
the presence of a bilobed gallbladder, r: The presence of a bilobed gallbladder, related to a duplication of the gallbladder primordium
absence or underdevelopment of the femur: Absence or underdevelopment of the femur
an abnormally increased rate of skeletal: An abnormally increased rate of skeletal maturation. Accelerated skeletal maturation can be diagnosed on the basis of an estimation of the bone age from radiographs of specific bones in the human body
hyperconvexity of the thoracolumbar spin: Hyperconvexity of the thoracolumbar spine producing a rounded or humped appearance
the ability of the interphalangeal joint: The ability of the interphalangeal joints to move beyond their normal range of motion
increased cross-section (diameter) of th: Increased cross-section (diameter) of the long bones. Note that widening may primarily affect specific regions of long bones (e.g., diaphysis or metaphysis), but this should be coded separately
absent ossification of the calvaria (vau: Absent ossification of the calvaria (vault of the skull)
bony fusion of the posterior part of the: Bony fusion of the posterior part of the L5 vertebral body with the sacrum
this type of brachydactyly is characteri: This type of brachydactyly is characterized by short and broad terminal phalanges of the thumbs and big toes
the ability of the joints of the hand to: The ability of the joints of the hand to move beyond their normal range of motion
the presence of abnormal calcium deposit: The presence of abnormal calcium deposition of the intervertebral disk
underdevelopment of the ulna on both sid: Underdevelopment of the ulna on both sides
a soft tissue continuity in the anteropo: A soft tissue continuity in the anteroposterior axis between the second to the fifth fingers that extends distally to at least the level of the proximal interphalangeal joints
sclerosis (abnormal hardening) of cortic: Sclerosis (abnormal hardening) of cortical bone, characterized by increased radiodensity
multiple exostoses originating in the fi: Multiple exostoses originating in the fingers and toes
a foot deformity resulting due to an abn: A foot deformity resulting due to an abnormality affecting the muscle and soft tissue. In contrast if the bones of the foot are affected the term structural foot deformity applies
osteomyelitis caused by infection with t: Osteomyelitis caused by infection with the bacteria, salmonella
extreme thickening of the cortex of long: Extreme thickening of the cortex of long bones
separation of the odontoid process from: Separation of the odontoid process from the body of the axis
deposition of calcium salts on both side: Deposition of calcium salts on both sides of the brain
an abnormality of the hand resulting fro: An abnormality of the hand resulting from contracture of the palmar fascia with a fixed flexion deformity of the metacarpophalangeal (MCP) joints and the proximal interphalangeal (PIP) joints
a tongue-like protusion from the anterio: A tongue-like protusion from the anterior aspect of lumbar vertebral bodies
a form of arthrogryposis primarily affec: A form of arthrogryposis primarily affecting the hands and the feet
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
increased mobility and flexibility in th: Increased mobility and flexibility in the joint due to the tension in tissues such as ligaments and muscles
supernumerary digits located at the ulna: Supernumerary digits located at the ulnar side of the hand with a complete extra finger and extra metacarpal
a bilateral form of triphalangeal thumb: A bilateral form of triphalangeal thumb
abnormal reduction in length of all meta: Abnormal reduction in length of all metacarpal bones
the ability of the thumb joints to move: The ability of the thumb joints to move beyond their normal range of motion
a form of triphalangeal thumb that canno: A form of triphalangeal thumb that cannot be placed opposite the fingers of the same hand
an abnormal irregularity of cortical bon: An abnormal irregularity of cortical bone
an abnormal narrowing of the spinal cana: An abnormal narrowing of the spinal canal related to a reduction in the interpedicular distance (i.e., the distance measured between the pedicles on frontal [coronal] imaging)
partial dislocation of the head of the r: Partial dislocation of the head of the radius in the posterior direction
avascular necrosis of the proximal epiph: Avascular necrosis of the proximal epiphysis of the femur occurring in growing children and caused by an interruption of the blood supply to the head of the femur close to the hip joint. The necrosis is characteristically associated with flattening of the femoral head, for which reason the term coxa plana has been used to refer to this feature in the medical literature
an increase in bone density affecting th: An increase in bone density affecting the basicranium (base of the skull)
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in one or more epiphyses during the neonatal period
abnormal elevation of the floor of the p: Abnormal elevation of the floor of the posterior fossa including occipital condyles and foramen magnum
an abnormally small and flat configurati: An abnormally small and flat configuration of the posterior cranial fossa
a soft tissue continuity in the anteropo: A soft tissue continuity in the anteroposterior axis between the toes 2, 3, and 4
bending or curvature of the distal phala: Bending or curvature of the distal phalanx of little finger in the radial direction (i.e., towards the 4th finger)
absence or underdevelopment of the tibia: Absence or underdevelopment of the tibia
underdevelopment of both forearm bones,: Underdevelopment of both forearm bones, the ulna and the radius, resulting in a shortened forearm
absence of the distal interphalangeal fl: Absence of the distal interphalangeal flexion creases of the fourth finger
a flattened vertebral body shape with re: A flattened vertebral body shape with reduced distance beween the vertebral endplates affecting the lumbar spine
an abnormal increase of bone mineral den: An abnormal increase of bone mineral density with generalized involvement of the skeleton
abnormal thickening of the cortex of the: Abnormal thickening of the cortex of the diaphyseal region of long bones
abnormally short distal phalanx of toe o: Abnormally short distal phalanx of toe of all toes
a dislocation of the head of the radius: A dislocation of the head of the radius from its socket in the elbow joint in an posterior direction
the presence of more than 12 rib pairs: The presence of more than 12 rib pairs
combined syndactyly and polydactyly of t: Combined syndactyly and polydactyly of the foot on the lateral side (i.e., on the side of the little toe)
short (hypoplastic) middle phalanx of fi: Short (hypoplastic) middle phalanx of finger, affecting one or more fingers
bending or curvature of a second toe in: Bending or curvature of a second toe in the tibial direction (i.e., towards the big toe)
one or more bent (flexed) toe joints tha: One or more bent (flexed) toe joints that cannot be straightened actively or passively
a type of dysharmonic skeletal maturatio: A type of dysharmonic skeletal maturation in which there is a delay in skeletal maturation whose degree differs markedly in different bones
an abnormally round shape of the middle: An abnormally round shape of the middle phalanx of the finger
cleft (split into two parts) distal phal: Cleft (split into two parts) distal phalanx of thumb
generalized deposition of calcium salts: Generalized deposition of calcium salts within the brain
the presence of bone fractures in the pr: The presence of bone fractures in the prenatal period that are diagnosed at birth or before
a dislocation of the head of the radius: A dislocation of the head of the radius from its socket in the elbow joint in an ulnar direction
in type e brachydactyly, shortening of t: In type E brachydactyly, shortening of the fingers is mainly in the metacarpals and metatarsals
a pathologic entity characterized by a d: A pathologic entity characterized by a developmental defect in a long bone leading to bending and pathologic fracture, with inability to form a normal bony callus with subsequent fibrous nonunion, leading to the pseudarthrosis (or "false joint")
a form of triphalangeal thumb that can b: A form of triphalangeal thumb that can be placed opposite the fingers of the same hand
an enchondroma is a benign growth of car: An enchondroma is a benign growth of cartilage that develops within the medullary cavity of bone. Enchondromatosis refers to the presence of multiple enchondromas, and this term refers to the presence of multiple enchondromas within the medulla of metaphyseal bone. Radiographically an enchondroma presents a an oval, linear, or pyramidal osteolytic (radiolucent) lesion with well defined margins
an abnormality of skeletal development c: An abnormality of skeletal development characterized by a disturbance of the metaphysis and its histological structure with relatively normal epiphyses and vertebrae
disproportionately short middle and dist: Disproportionately short middle and distal phalanges compared to the hand/foot
combined syndactyly and polydactyly of t: Combined syndactyly and polydactyly of the great toe
multiple bent (flexed) finger joints tha: Multiple bent (flexed) finger joints that cannot be straightened actively or passively
fusion of a metacarpal bone with the pro: Fusion of a metacarpal bone with the proximal phalanx of the finger distal to it across the corresponding metacarpophalangeal joint
a lack of bone mineralization of one or: A lack of bone mineralization of one or more body of cervical vertebra
absence of a digit or of one or more pha: Absence of a digit or of one or more phalanges of a finger
bony overgrowth of the internal (endoste: Bony overgrowth of the internal (endosteal) surface of the calvaria and the base of skull
progressive bending or abnormal curvatur: Progressive bending or abnormal curvature of the forearm skeleton
duplication of the metacarpal i bones: Duplication of the metacarpal I bones
abnormal mineralization of the metaphyse: Abnormal mineralization of the metaphyseal area of bones
presence of an angular or v -shaped inde: Presence of an angular or V -shaped indentation on the ulnar side of the fifth metacarpal bone (i.e., on the sides towards the fifth finger)
the presence of an abnormal curvature of: The presence of an abnormal curvature of the cervical vertebral column
an abnormally reduced degree of pneumati: An abnormally reduced degree of pneumatization (i.e., formation of air cells) in the mastoid process with respect to age-dependent norms
rhomboid or triangular shaped 5th (littl: Rhomboid or triangular shaped 5th (little) finger middle phalanx
aplasia or hypoplasia affecting the meta: Aplasia or Hypoplasia affecting the metacarpal bones
irregularly shaped metacarpal bones of v: Irregularly shaped metacarpal bones of varying degree
the presence of more than the normal num: The presence of more than the normal number of metacarpal bones
abnormalities affecting the phalanx of f: Abnormalities affecting the phalanx of finger
abnormality of one or all of the epiphys: Abnormality of one or all of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the hand. Note that this includes the epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal. In contrast to the metacarpals 2-5, the first metacarpal is embryologically of phalangeal origin and as such equivalent to the proximal phalanges of the digits 2-5 (whereas the proximal phalanx of the thumb is equivalent to the middle phalanges of the other digits)
any structural anomaly of the hand: Any structural anomaly of the hand
any abnormality of the epiphyses of the: Any abnormality of the epiphyses of the phalanges or metacarpal bones
an anomaly of the outer shell (cortex) o: An anomaly of the outer shell (cortex) of a hand bone
absence (due to failure to form) or unde: Absence (due to failure to form) or underdevelopment of the bones of the hand
an anomaly of epiphysis, which is the ex: An anomaly of epiphysis, which is the expanded articular end of a long bone that developes from a secondary ossification center, and which during the period of growth is either entirely cartilaginous or is separated from the shaft by a cartilaginous disk
an abnormality of corticomedullary diffe: An abnormality of corticomedullary differentiation (CMD) on diagnostic imaging such as magnetic resonance imaging, computer tomography, or sonography. CMD is a difference in the visualization of cortex and medulla
abnormal arrangement of the structures o: Abnormal arrangement of the structures of the motile cilium
bilateral lack of development of the lun: Bilateral lack of development of the lungs
blockage of the upper airway at the leve: Blockage of the upper airway at the level of the larynx often accompanied by respiratory distress
reduced tendency to respond to a reduced: Reduced tendency to respond to a reduced concentration of oxygen in the blood by increasing respiration
recurrent episodes of apnea occurring du: Recurrent episodes of apnea occurring during infancy
a membrane-like structure that extends a: A membrane-like structure that extends across the laryngeal lumen close to the level of the vocal cords
abnormal shortening of the larynx in the: Abnormal shortening of the larynx in the anteroposterior (front to back) axis
a decreased concentration of arginine in: A decreased concentration of arginine in the blood
disordered thermoregulation characterize: Disordered thermoregulation characterized by an impaired ability to maintain a balance between heat production and heat loss, with resulting instability of body temperature
acidosis because of respiratory retentio: Acidosis because of respiratory retention of carbon dioxide
a type of diabetes mellitus initially ch: A type of diabetes mellitus initially characterized by insulin resistance and hyperinsulinemia and subsequently by glucose interolerance and hyperglycemia
an elevation of alpha-feto protein in th: An elevation of alpha-feto protein in the maternal serum
enlargement of the thyroid gland related: Enlargement of the thyroid gland related to multiple nodules in the thyroid gland
a congenital form of torticollis resulti: A congenital form of torticollis resulting from shortening of the sternocleidomastoid muscle and leading to a limited range of motion in both rotation and lateral bending
excess skin around the neck, often lying: Excess skin around the neck, often lying in horizontal folds
developmental hypoplasia of the thyroid: Developmental hypoplasia of the thyroid gland
enlargement of the thyroid gland related: Enlargement of the thyroid gland related to one or more nodules in the thyroid gland
reduced amount of adipose tissue in the: Reduced amount of adipose tissue in the region of the neck
obstruction of the flow of urine through: Obstruction of the flow of urine through the ureter
increased side-to-side width of one or m: Increased side-to-side width of one or more phalanges of the fingers or toes
severely shortened metacarpal with a cub: Severely shortened metacarpal with a cuboidal appearance
delay in the process of formation and ma: Delay in the process of formation and maturation of the epiphysis of one or more phalanx
metaphyseal cupping affecting the metaca: Metaphyseal cupping affecting the metacarpal bones
y-shaped metacarpals are the result of a: Y-shaped metacarpals are the result of a partial fusion of two metacarpal bones, with the two arms of the Y pointing in the distal direction. Y-shaped metacarpals may be seen in combination with polydactyly
abnormal increase in width of the distal: Abnormal increase in width of the distal region of the metacarpal bones
a cone-shaped appearance of the epiphyse: A cone-shaped appearance of the epiphyses of the metacarpal bones, producing a 'ball-in-a-socket' appearance. This epiphyses are located at the distal ends of the metacarpal bones
a delay in the process of formation and: A delay in the process of formation and maturation of the epiphysis of one or more long bones
a chronic loss of joint motion in metaca: A chronic loss of joint motion in metacarpophalangeal joints due to structural changes in muscle, tendons, ligaments, or skin that prevents normal movement
absence of the proximal interphalangeal: Absence of the proximal interphalangeal flexion creases of the fingers
malalignement of carpal bone angles eith: Malalignement of carpal bone angles either with respect to each other, to the corresponding metacarpals or with respect to the wrist (radius and ulna)
syndactyly with fusion of fingers three: Syndactyly with fusion of fingers three and four
increased mobility of one ore more metac: Increased mobility of one ore more metacarpophalangeal joint
webbing or fusion of the fingers, involv: Webbing or fusion of the fingers, involving soft parts only or including bone structure. Bony fusions are referred to as "bony" Syndactyly if the fusion occurs in a radio-ulnar axis. Fusions of bones of the fingers in a proximo-distal axis are referred to as "Symphalangism"
telangiectasia (small dilated blood vess: Telangiectasia (small dilated blood vessels) located in the fingerpads at the tips of the fingers
metacarpal that becomes thinner toward t: Metacarpal that becomes thinner toward the distal end
absence of one or more interphalangeal c: Absence of one or more interphalangeal creases (i.e., of the transverse lines in the skin between the phalanges of the fingers)
short (hypoplastic middle phalanx of fin: Short (hypoplastic middle phalanx of finger, affecting all fingers
the presence of multiple creases on the: The presence of multiple creases on the palm of the hand (more than the normal three major creases (distal transverse crease, proximal transverse crease, and thenar crease)
hypoplasia of all of the distal phalanx: Hypoplasia of all of the distal phalanx of finger
some or all of the metacarpal bones (i.e: Some or all of the metacarpal bones (i.e., metacarpal II to V) have a pointed proximal appearance
a type of digital ulcer that manifests a: A type of digital ulcer that manifests as an open sore on the surface of the skin at the tip of a finger or toe
increased length of the proximal phalanx: Increased length of the proximal phalanx of finger
rounding and broadening of the tufts of: Rounding and broadening of the tufts of the distal phalanges
abnormally large size of the metaphyseal: Abnormally large size of the metaphyseal epiphyses
fusion of the epiphysis and metaphysis o: Fusion of the epiphysis and metaphysis of one or more phalanges prior to the normal age or stage of growth
congenital hypoplasia of proximal phalan: Congenital hypoplasia of proximal phalanx of finger or all fingers
a central y-shaped metacarpal is the res: A central Y-shaped metacarpal is the result of a partial fusion of two central metacarpals (i.e., metacarpals 2-4) of the hand, with the two arms of the Y pointing in the distal direction. Central Y-shaped metacarpals may be seen as a result of a central polydactyly with partial fusion of the duplicated metacarpal
increased side-to-side width of the meta: Increased side-to-side width of the metacarpal epiphyses
a swan neck deformity describes a finger: A swan neck deformity describes a finger with a hyperextended PIP joint and a flexed DIP joint. The most common cause for a swan neck-like deformity is a disruption of the end of the extensor tendon. Conditions that loosen the PIP joint and allow it to hyperextend, for example conditions that weaken the volar plate, can produce a swan neck deformity of the finger. One example is rheumatoid arthritis. Another cause are conditions that tighten up the small (intrinsic) muscles of the hand and fingers, for example hand trauma or nerve disorders, such as cerebral palsy, Parkinson's disease, or stroke
the term proximal symphalangism refers t: The term proximal symphalangism refers to a bony fusion of the middle and proximal phalanges of the digits of the hand, in other words the proximal interphalangeal joint (PIJ) is missing which can be seen either on x-rays or as an absence of the proximal interphalangeal finger creases
increased length of multiple or a single: Increased length of multiple or a single phalanx of finger
bending or curvature of a thumb towards: Bending or curvature of a thumb towards the ulnar side (towards the ring finger)
the presence of a supernumerary finger (: The presence of a supernumerary finger (not a thumb) involving the third or fourth metacarpal with associated osseous syndactyly
irregular morphology of one or more meta: Irregular morphology of one or more metacarpal bones
a morphological abnormality of distal ph: A morphological abnormality of distal phalanges such that they have the appearance of chess pawns
increase in bone denisty in the diaphyse: Increase in bone denisty in the diaphyseal (shaft) region of a metacarpal bone
poorly defined or shallow palmar creases: Poorly defined or shallow palmar creases
excessively deep creases of the palm: Excessively deep creases of the palm
phalanges of the fingers becoming thinne: Phalanges of the fingers becoming thinner toward the distal end
the middle phalanx of finger resembles a: The middle phalanx of finger resembles a thimble, a small metal cap to protect the finger while sewing that has a broad (proximal) base and narrower top, whereby both base and top are flat
alteration of the normally smooth radiog: Alteration of the normally smooth radiographic contour of phalanges producing an irregular appearance
presence of an additional phalanx-like b: Presence of an additional phalanx-like bone, producing an extra, wedge-shaped bone at the base of the proximal phalanx of the second finger
metaphyseal cupping affecting the proxim: Metaphyseal cupping affecting the proximal phalanges
presence of only one (instead of two, as: Presence of only one (instead of two, as normal) interphalangeal crease of the fifth finger
a reduction in diameter of the distal ph: A reduction in diameter of the distal phalanx of finger towards the distal end such that the tip of the phalanx comes to a point (this feature can be observed on radiograms)
an increased resorption of bone matrix b: An increased resorption of bone matrix by osteoclasts leading to bony defects involving the tarsal bones
decreased width of the metacarpal bones: Decreased width of the metacarpal bones (that is, reduced diameter)
abnormal shortening of all middle phalan: Abnormal shortening of all middle phalanges of toes
an abnormal limitation of the mobility o: An abnormal limitation of the mobility of the wrist
abnormality of one or all of the epiphys: Abnormality of one or all of the epiphyses of the proximal, middle, and distal phalanges of the 2nd finger
intermittently increased size of the spl: Intermittently increased size of the spleen
the presence of lymphangiectasis in the: The presence of lymphangiectasis in the pancreas
reduced number of beta cells in the panc: Reduced number of beta cells in the pancreatic islets of Langerhans
the presence of pancreatic tissue outsid: The presence of pancreatic tissue outside the normal pancreas, in many cases along the foregut and proximal midgut
a generalized form of developmental hypo: A generalized form of developmental hypoplasia of the dental enamel
the presence of multiple embedded tooth: The presence of multiple embedded tooth germs which have failed to erupt
a decreased amount of enamel mineralizat: A decreased amount of enamel mineralization
premature tooth eruption, which can be d: Premature tooth eruption, which can be defined as tooth eruption more than 2 SD earlier than the mean eruption age
agenesis of one or more central incisors: Agenesis of one or more central incisors, i.e., of lower secondary incisor, lower primary incisor, upper secondary incisor, or of upper central primary incisor
the presence of discolored lateral incis: The presence of discolored lateral incisors
agenesis of upper secondary incisor or o: Agenesis of upper secondary incisor or of upper central primary incisor
developmental hypoplasia of the dental e: Developmental hypoplasia of the dental enamel
prolonged bleeding post dental extractio: Prolonged bleeding post dental extraction sufficient to require medical intervention
dagger-shaped calcifications in the dent: Dagger-shaped calcifications in the dental pulp
increased space between the primary teet: Increased space between the primary teeth. Note this phenotype should be distinguished from increased space due purely to microdontia
the presence of a single, median maxilla: The presence of a single, median maxillary incisor, affecting both the primary maxillary incisor and the permanent maxillary incisor
irregular distribution of the teeth alon: Irregular distribution of the teeth along the dental arch, i.e., and irregular spatial pattern of teeth
loss of the primary (also known as decid: Loss of the primary (also known as deciduous) teeth before the usual age
underdevelopment of the alveolar process: Underdevelopment of the alveolar process (also known as alveolar bone)
the presence of a supernumerary, i.e., e: The presence of a supernumerary, i.e., extra, maxillary incisor, either the primary maxillary incisor or the permanent maxillary incisor
a type of dental misalignment with crowd: A type of dental misalignment with crowded central incisors, i.e., of maxillary secondary incisor, or of maxillary central primary incisor
developmental hypoplasia of the primary: Developmental hypoplasia of the primary teeth
persistence of the primary teeth beyond: Persistence of the primary teeth beyond the age by which they normally are shed and replaced by the permanent teeth
premature tooth eruption of the permanen: Premature tooth eruption of the permanent dentition
an abnormality of themorphology of secon: An abnormality of themorphology of secondary premolar tooth
an abnormal conical morphology of the pr: An abnormal conical morphology of the primary or permanent mandibular incisors
peg-shaped upper lateral secondary incis: Peg-shaped upper lateral secondary incisor tooth
an abnormality of morphology of primary: An abnormality of morphology of primary molar
an abnormality of morphology of the inci: An abnormality of morphology of the incisor tooth in which the tooth is shaped like a screwdriver blade, i.e., having a rhomboid shape
decreased size of the primary teeth: Decreased size of the primary teeth
a congenital defect characterized by the: A congenital defect characterized by the absence of one or more permanent teeth, including oligodontia, hypodontia, and adontia of the of permanent teeth
obliteration of the pulp chambers owing: Obliteration of the pulp chambers owing to mineralization of the dental pulp
lack of tooth eruption of the secondary: Lack of tooth eruption of the secondary dentition
developmental hypoplasia of the tooth ge: Developmental hypoplasia of the tooth germ, i.e., of the structure that forms in odontogenesis that will develop into a tooth
agenesis of lower secondary incisor or l: Agenesis of lower secondary incisor or lower primary incisor
premature loss of the permanent teeth: Premature loss of the permanent teeth
incisors with a thick marginal ridge sur: Incisors with a thick marginal ridge surrounding a deep lingual fossa are termed shovel-shaped incisors
an crumpled radiographic appearance of t: An crumpled radiographic appearance of the long bones, as is the long bone had been crushed together producing irregularities. This feature is the result of multiple fractures and repeated rounds of ineffective healing, as can be seen for instance in severe forms of osteogenesis imperfecta
an alteration of the normally relatively: An alteration of the normally relatively smooth margins of the kneecap in radiographic images leading to an irregular contour
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in the distal epiphysis of the ulna
increased width of the diaphysis of long: Increased width of the diaphysis of long bones
the femur is shortened and displays flar: The femur is shortened and displays flaring (widening) of the metaphyses
a bent (flexed) knee joint that cannot b: A bent (flexed) knee joint that cannot be straightened actively or passively
absent or nearly absent fibula. (does no: Absent or nearly absent fibula. (Does not include aplastic)
progressive bending or abnormal curvatur: Progressive bending or abnormal curvature of a long bone
an abnormal conformation of the femur th: An abnormal conformation of the femur that becomes gradually enlarged towards the distal end. This feature affects the distal femoral metaphysis and epiphysis
shortening of the legs related to develo: Shortening of the legs related to developmental hypoplasia of the bones of the leg
underdevelopment of the distal epiphysis: Underdevelopment of the distal epiphysis of the radius
increased width of the distal part of th: Increased width of the distal part of the shaft (metaphysis) of the femur
an abnormal anterior bending or curvatur: An abnormal anterior bending or curvature of the tibia
overconstriction, or narrowness of the d: Overconstriction, or narrowness of the diaphysis and metaphysis of long bones
an abnormal increase in the bone density: An abnormal increase in the bone density of the long bones
a limitation of the ability to place the: A limitation of the ability to place the forearm in a position such that the palm faces anteriorly (supination) and to place the forearm in a position such that the palm faces posteriorly (pronation)
an abnormal flattening of the distal epi: An abnormal flattening of the distal epiphysis of femur
an abnormal conformation of the femur th: An abnormal conformation of the femur that becomes gradually enlarged towards the proximal end. This feature affects the proximal femoral metaphysis and epiphysis
anomaly of the contour of the distal epi: Anomaly of the contour of the Distal epiphysis of femur such that its normally smooth appearance is irregular
progressive bending or abnormal curvatur: Progressive bending or abnormal curvature of the leg
a bending or abnormal curvature of the d: A bending or abnormal curvature of the distal portion of the tibia
increased density of the compact bone of: Increased density of the compact bone of long bone
the presence of asymmetric developmental: The presence of asymmetric developmental dysplasia of the radius
an abnormally wide femoral neck (which i: An abnormally wide femoral neck (which is the process of bone, connecting the femoral head with the femoral shaft)
an anomaly of the metaphysis of the prox: An anomaly of the metaphysis of the proximal femur (close to the hip)
proximal radial shortening owing to a co: Proximal radial shortening owing to a congenital defect of development
an abnormal enlargement of the distal ep: An abnormal enlargement of the distal epiphysis of the femur
a developmental anomaly with lateral dis: A developmental anomaly with lateral displacement of the femoral head
formation of bone in the patella later t: Formation of bone in the patella later than normal
anomaly of the contour of the proximal e: Anomaly of the contour of the proximal epiphysis of the tibia such that its normally smooth appearance is irregular
partial dislocation of the ulna in the d: Partial dislocation of the ulna in the dorsal direction
slipped capital femoral epiphysis is def: Slipped capital femoral epiphysis is defined as a posterior and inferior slippage of the proximal epiphysis of the femur onto the metaphysis (femoral neck), occurring through the physeal plate during the early adolescent growth spurt
a generalized decrease in bone mineral d: A generalized decrease in bone mineral density
thickening of the periosteum of long bon: Thickening of the periosteum of long bone
a chronic loss of ankle joint motion due: A chronic loss of ankle joint motion due to structural changes in muscle, tendons, ligaments, or skin that prevent normal movement of the joints of the ankle
a limitation of the range of movement of: A limitation of the range of movement of the shoulder joint
decreased width of the diaphysis of long: Decreased width of the diaphysis of long bones
an abnormal anterior curvature of a long: An abnormal anterior curvature of a long bone
any abnormality of the alveolar ridges (: Any abnormality of the alveolar ridges (on the upper or lower jaws). The alveolar ridges contain the sockets (alveoli) of the teeth
an abnormality of the dental pulp: An abnormality of the dental pulp
premature loss of teeth not related to t: Premature loss of teeth not related to trauma or neglect
any abnormality of the primary tooth: Any abnormality of the primary tooth
an abnormality of the morphology of the: An abnormality of the morphology of the tooth
the presence of an altered number of of: The presence of an altered number of of teeth

a bending or abnormal curvature of a lon: A bending or abnormal curvature of a long bone
a bending or abnormal curvature affectin: A bending or abnormal curvature affecting a long bone of the arm
an anomaly of the femoral metaphysis: An anomaly of the femoral metaphysis
absence or underdevelopment of the fibul: Absence or underdevelopment of the fibula
absence (due to failure to form) or unde: Absence (due to failure to form) or underdevelopment of the bones of the lower limbs
absence or underdevelopment of the ulna: Absence or underdevelopment of the ulna
absence (due to failure to form) or unde: Absence (due to failure to form) or underdevelopment of the bones of the upper limbs
absence or underdevelopment of the patel: Absence or underdevelopment of the patella
an anomaly of a growth plate of a femur: An anomaly of a growth plate of a femur
an anomaly of one or more epiphyses of o: An anomaly of one or more epiphyses of one or both legs
absence or underdevelopment of the carpa: Absence or underdevelopment of the carpal bones
absence (due to failure to form) or unde: Absence (due to failure to form) or underdevelopment of one or more forearm bones
a structural abnormality of a diaphysis: A structural abnormality of a diaphysis of the arm or leg
an anomaly of one or more epiphyses of a: An anomaly of one or more epiphyses of a limb
absence (due to failure to form) or unde: Absence (due to failure to form) or underdevelopment of the humerus
an anomaly that is characterized progres: An anomaly that is characterized progressive airflow obstruction that is only partly reversible, inflammation in the airways, and systemic effects or comorbities
an abnormal high-pitched noisy sound, oc: An abnormal high-pitched noisy sound, occurring during inhalation or exhalation caused by the incomplete obstruction in the throat
abnormal accumulation of surfactant-like: Abnormal accumulation of surfactant-like, periodic acid-schiff-positive lipoproteinaceous material in macrophages within the alveolar spaces and distal bronchioles. This results in gas exchange impairment leading to dyspnea and alveolar infiltrates
substantial narrowing or blockage of sma: Substantial narrowing or blockage of small pulmonary veins as a result of disorganized smooth muscle hypertrophy and collagen matrix deposition
abnormal dilatation of the pulmonary lym: Abnormal dilatation of the pulmonary lymphatic vessels. Lymphatic fluid in the lung is derived from normal leakage of fluid out of the blood capillaries in the lung. In pulmonary lymphangiectasia, the pulmonary lymphatics are not properly connected and become dilated with fluid
a lymphocyte-predominant infiltration of: A lymphocyte-predominant infiltration of the lungs characterized by bibasilar pulmonary infiltrates with dense interstitial accumulations of lymphocytes and plasma cells
according to the definitions of the amer: According to the definitions of the American and British Thoracic Societies, including pulmonary functional tests, X-rays, and CT scans for items such as fibrosis, bronchiectasis, bullae, emphysema, nodular or lymphomatous abnormalities
an abnormality of the pulmonary lymphati: An abnormality of the pulmonary lymphatic chain
abnormality of the lung parenchyma exten: Abnormality of the lung parenchyma extending to the pulmonary interstitium and leading to diffuse pulmonary fibrosis
an increased susceptibility to pneumonia: An increased susceptibility to pneumonia as manifested by a history of recurrent episodes of pneumonia
a recurrent hemorrhage occurring within: A recurrent hemorrhage occurring within the lung
obstruction of conducting airways of the: Obstruction of conducting airways of the lung

hepatic failure refers to the inability: Hepatic failure refers to the inability of the liver to perform its normal synthetic and metabolic functions, which can result in coagulopathy and alteration in the mental status of a previously healthy individual. Hepatic failure is defined as acute if there is onset of encephalopathy within 8 weeks of the onset of symptoms in a patient with a previously healthy liver

neonatal jaundice refers to a yellowing: Neonatal jaundice refers to a yellowing of the skin and other tissues of a newborn infant as a result of increased concentrations of bilirubin in the blood. Neonatal jaundice affects over half of all newborns to some extent in the first week of life. Prolonged neonatal jaundice is said to be present if the jaundice persists for longer than 14 days in term infants and 21 days in preterm infants

repeated occurrences of acute noninflamm: Repeated occurrences of acute noninflammatory encephalopathy and fatty degenerative liver failure
the two portions of the clavicle (corres: The two portions of the clavicle (corresponding to the two primary ossification centers of the clavicle) are connected by a fibrous bridge that is contiguous with the periosteum, and a synovial membrane develops, resulting in a clavicle with a bipartite appearance radiographically. Congenital pseudarthrosis of the clavicle generally presents as a painless mass or swelling over the clavicle
an abnormally straight configuration of: An abnormally straight configuration of the clavicle, a tubular bone which normally is doubly curved
lack of development of the glenoid fossa: Lack of development of the glenoid fossa, also known as the glenoid cavity, which is the articular surface of the scapula that articulates with the head of the humerus

the presence of a paralyzed diaphragm: The presence of a paralyzed diaphragm
irregular surface of the normally relati: Irregular surface of the normally relatively smooth border between the distal part of the ribs and the costal cartilages, which are bars of hyaline cartilage that connect the ribs to the sternum

underdevelopment of the middle portion o: Underdevelopment of the middle portion of the clavicle
a larger than usual distance between the: A larger than usual distance between the left and right nipple
a less than normal number of sternal oss: A less than normal number of sternal ossification centers. The sternum is initially formed from bilateral sternal plates that chondrify and begin to fuse with ribs at 10 weeks gestational age. Ossification starts in the manubrium and upper part of the sternal body at the 6th month, in the middle of the sternal body at the 7th month, in the lower part of the body during the 1st postnatal year and in the xiphoid process between years 5 and 18. The number of ossification centers vary up to six, and it is the ossification centers that are visualized by prenatal ultrasound. This term describes a reduction in the number of ossification centers compared with age-related norms
lack of formation and mineralization of: Lack of formation and mineralization of the ribs in utero

osteosclerosis of ribs (increased densit: Osteosclerosis of ribs (increased density related to increased bone mass)
developmental defect resulting in congen: Developmental defect resulting in congenital absence of the middle portion of the clavicle
more than one fracture of the ribs: More than one fracture of the ribs
abnormally reduced diameter (cross secti: Abnormally reduced diameter (cross section) of the clavicles
calcification of the costal cartilages,: Calcification of the costal cartilages, which are bars of hyaline cartilage found at the anterior ends of the ribs which serve to prolong the ribs forward and contribute to the elasticity of the walls of the thorax

increased time for the complex comprised: Increased time for the complex comprised of the Q wave, R wave, and S wave as measured by the electrocardiogram (EKG).. In adults, normal values are 0.06 - 0.10 sec
inflammation of the coronary arteries in: Inflammation of the coronary arteries involving a granulomatous response, i.e., a non-specific inflammatory response involving granulomas, defined as a compact organized collection of mature mononuclear phagocytes including epithelioid and giant cells
premature ventricular contractions (pvc): Premature ventricular contractions (PVC) or ventricular extrasystoles are premature contractions of the heart that arise in response to an impulse in the ventricles rather than the normal impulse from the sinoatrial (SA) node
an abnormality of filling of a ventricle: An abnormality of filling of a ventricle with blood during diastole

acute lymphoblastic leukemia of t-cell o: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia of T-cell origin. It comprises about 15% of childhood cases and 25% of adult cases. It is more common in males than females
the presence of an follicular adenocarci: The presence of an follicular adenocarcinoma of the thyroid gland
a type of papillary renal cell carcinoma: A type of papillary renal cell carcinoma in which the papillae are covered by large eosinophilic cells with pleomorphic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and nuclear pseudostratification
a rare subtype of acute myeloid leukemia: A rare subtype of acute myeloid leukemia evolving from primitive megakaryoblasts
the presence of an adenoma in the cortex: The presence of an adenoma in the cortex of the kidney

borderline intellectual disability is de: Borderline intellectual disability is defined as an intelligence quotient (IQ) in the range of 70-85

brain damage related to a lowering of bl: Brain damage related to a lowering of blood glucose below a critical level (around 30 mg/dl), which may lead to confusion, lethargy and delirium followed by seizures and coma. Prolonged hypoglycemia may lead to irreversible brain damage

nystagmus dating from or present at birt: Nystagmus dating from or present at birth
a reduced sense of touch (tactile sensat: A reduced sense of touch (tactile sensation) on the skin of the distal limbs. This is usually tested with a wisp of cotton or a fine camel's hair brush, by asking patients to say 'now' each time they feel the stimulus

dysplasia and dysgenesis of the corpus c: Dysplasia and dysgenesis of the corpus callosum are nonspecific descriptions that imply defective development of the corpus callosum. The term dysplasia is applied when the morphology of the corpus callosum is altered as a congenital trait. For instance, the corpus callosum may be hump-shaped, kinked, or a striped corpus callosum that lacks an anatomically distinct genu and splenium

progressive impairment of function of mo: Progressive impairment of function of motor axons with muscle weakness, atrophy, and cramps. the formation of small cavities in the t: The formation of small cavities in the tissue of the basal ganglia
an abnormality of the ability (skills) t: An abnormality of the ability (skills) to perform a precise movement of small muscles with the intent to perform a specific act. Fine motor skills are required to mediate movements of the wrists, hands, fingers, feet, and toes

attention deficit hyperactivity disorder: Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) manifests at age 2-3 years or by first grade at the latest. The main symptoms are distractibility, impulsivity, hyperactivity, and often trouble organizing tasks and projects, difficulty going to sleep, and social problems from being aggressive, loud, or impatient
cerebral hemorrhage that occurs before b: Cerebral hemorrhage that occurs before birth
bilateral impairment of the function of: Bilateral impairment of the function of the cranial nerves 9-12, which control musculature involved in eating, swallowing, and speech. Pseudobulbar paralysis is characterized clinically by dysarthria, dysphonia, and dysphagia with bifacial paralysis, and may be accompanied by Pseudobulbar behavioral symptoms such as enforced crying and laughing
a morphological abnormality of the meten: A morphological abnormality of the metencephalon

episodes of choreoathetosis that can occ: Episodes of choreoathetosis that can occur following triggers such as quick voluntary movements
arnold-chiari type i malformation refers: Arnold-Chiari type I malformation refers to a relatively mild degree of herniation of the posteroinferior region of the cerebellum (the cerebellar tonsils) into the cervical canal with little or no displacement of the fourth ventricle

encephalopathy with onset in the infanti: Encephalopathy with onset in the infantile period
