absence of one or more of the phalanges: Absence of one or more of the phalanges of the hand
short (hypoplastic) phalanx of finger, a: Short (hypoplastic) phalanx of finger, affecting one or more phalanges
the presence of a reduced number of teet: The presence of a reduced number of teeth as in Hypodontia or as in Anodontia
a form of heart failure characterized by: A form of heart failure characterized by reduced cardiac output. This may be seen in patients with heart failure owing to ischemic heart disease, hypertension, cardiomyopathy, and other causes
a form of diabetes insipidus caused by f: A form of diabetes insipidus caused by failure of the kidneys to respond to vasopressin (AVP)
a structural abnormality of a diaphysis: A structural abnormality of a diaphysis of the arm
an anomaly of one or more metaphyses of: An anomaly of one or more metaphyses of the arms
an anomaly of the joint that connects th: An anomaly of the joint that connects the upper and the lower arm
amelia of one or both upper limbs: Amelia of one or both upper limbs
missing or malformed long bones of the u: Missing or malformed long bones of the upper limbs with the distal parts (the hands) connected to the variably shortened or even absent upper extremity, leading to a flipper-like appearance, as opposed to other forms of limb malformations were either the whole limb is missing (such as amelia), or the distal part of a limb is absent (peromelia)
peromelia affecting only the upper limbs: Peromelia affecting only the upper limbs. That is, the distal parts of the arm are missing leading to stump formation
absence (due to failure to form) or unde: Absence (due to failure to form) or underdevelopment of the extremities
leg shortening because of underdevelopme: Leg shortening because of underdevelopment of one or more bones of the lower extremity
amelia of one or both legs: Amelia of one or both legs
phocomelia affecting only the lower limb: Phocomelia affecting only the lower limbs
peromelia affecting only the lower limbs: Peromelia affecting only the lower limbs. That is, the distal parts of the leg are missing leading to stump formation
forearm shortening because of underdevel: Forearm shortening because of underdevelopment of one or more bones of the forearm
arm shortening because of underdevelopme: Arm shortening because of underdevelopment of one or more bones of the upper extremity
limb shortening because of underdevelopm: Limb shortening because of underdevelopment of one or more bones of the extremities
congenital absence (aplasia) of one or m: Congenital absence (aplasia) of one or more limbs
the distal parts of the limbs are missin: The distal parts of the limbs are missing leading to a stump formation
missing or malformed long bones of the e: Missing or malformed long bones of the extremities with the distal parts (such as hands and/or feet) connected to the variably shortened or even absent extremity, leading to a flipper-like appearance, as opposed to other forms of limb malformations were either the hole limb is missing (such as amelia), or the distal part of a limb is absent (peromelia)
peripheral neuropathy is a general term: Peripheral neuropathy is a general term for any disorder of the peripheral nervous system. The main clinical features used to classify peripheral neuropathy are distribution, type (mainly demyelinating versus mainly axonal), duration, and course
a focal lesion of a single peripheral ne: A focal lesion of a single peripheral nerve. Damage to a sensory nerve is accompanied by sensory impairment of all modalities in the affected anatomic distribution
any anomaly of distal phalanx of finger: Any anomaly of distal phalanx of finger
an anomaly of middle phalanx of finger: An anomaly of middle phalanx of finger
absence or underdevelopment of the dista: Absence or underdevelopment of the distal phalanges
abnormally wide (broad) distal phalanx o: Abnormally wide (broad) distal phalanx of finger
short and wide distal phalanges that tap: Short and wide distal phalanges that taper distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
uneven (irregular) increase in bone dens: Uneven (irregular) increase in bone density of the distal phalanges of the hand
increased width of the middle phalanx of: Increased width of the middle phalanx of finger
any of the middle phalanges with short a: Any of the middle phalanges with short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
uneven (irregular) increase in bone dens: Uneven (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the middle phalanges of the hand
fusion of a middle phalanx of a finger w: Fusion of a middle phalanx of a finger with another bone
increased width of the proximal phalange: Increased width of the proximal phalanges of the finger
short and wide proximal phalanges that t: Short and wide proximal phalanges that taper distally . Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
uneven increase in bone density of the p: Uneven increase in bone density of the proximal phalanges of the hand
the presence of cerebellar signs and sym: The presence of cerebellar signs and symptoms such as lack of balance associated with quadrupedal gait (locomotion on all four extremities with a 'bear-like' gait with the legs held straight)
an abnormal reduction of the number and: An abnormal reduction of the number and complexity of the pattern of gyrations of the cerebral cortex
abnormally wide (broad) distal phalanx o: Abnormally wide (broad) distal phalanx of finger of all fingers
short distance from the end of the finge: Short distance from the end of the finger to the most distal interphalangeal crease or the distal interphalangeal joint flexion point. That is, hypoplasia of one or more of the distal phalanx of finger
this term applies if one or more of the: This term applies if one or more of the distal phalanges of the hand are either partially duplicated, depending on severity leading to a broad or bifid appearance of the phalanges, or completely duplicated
a reduction in diameter of the distal ph: A reduction in diameter of the distal phalanx of finger towards the distal end
trichorrhexis nodosa is the formation of: Trichorrhexis nodosa is the formation of nodes along the hair shaft through which breakage readily occurs. It is thus a focal defect in the hair fiber that is characterized by thickening or weak points (nodes) that cause the hair to break off easily. The result is defective, abnormally fragile hair
abnormality of the growth of secondary s: Abnormality of the growth of secondary sexual hair, which normally ensues during puberty. In males, secondary sexual hair usually comprises body hair, including underarm, abdominal, chest, and pubic hair. In females, secondary sexual hair usually comprises a lesser degree of body hair, most prominently underarm and pubic hair
abnormally increased hair growth with a: Abnormally increased hair growth with a localized distribution
distance between the hairline (trichion): Distance between the hairline (trichion) and the glabella (the most prominent point on the frontal bone above the root of the nose), in the midline, more than two SD above the mean. Alternatively, an apparently increased distance between the hairline and the glabella
flatness of the supraorbital portion of: Flatness of the supraorbital portion of the frontal bones
complete absence of any auricular struct: Complete absence of any auricular structures
the presence of telangiectasia of the ea: The presence of telangiectasia of the ear
increased thickness of the external ear: Increased thickness of the external ear
an abnormality of the crus of the helix,: An abnormality of the crus of the helix, which is the horizontal piece of cartilage located outside the ear canal that divides the upper and lower parts of the ear
an abnormality of the antitragus, which: An abnormality of the antitragus, which is a small tubercle opposite to the tragus of the ear. The antitragus and the tragus are separated by the intertragic notch
an abnormal horizontal axis orientation: An abnormal horizontal axis orientation of the crus of the helix. That is, the main axis of the crus of the helix is perpendicular to the medial longitudinal axis of the ear, instead of sloping inferoposteriorly
developmental hypoplasia of the crus of: Developmental hypoplasia of the crus of the helix. That is, flatter and/or shorter crus helix than average
the presence of an abnormally prominent: The presence of an abnormally prominent of the crus of the helix. That is, development of the crus helix to the same degree as an average antihelix stem or helix
distortion of the course of the normal f: Distortion of the course of the normal folds of the ear and the appearance of supernumerary crura and folds
A notched form of the helix of the ear. That is, a defect in the continuity of the helix, which may occur at any point along its length
presence of nodules in the conjunctiva o: Presence of nodules in the conjunctiva of the eye
decreased thickness of the helix of the: Decreased thickness of the helix of the ear
absence or underdevelopment of the ear l: Absence or underdevelopment of the ear lobes
attachment of the lobe to the side of th: Attachment of the lobe to the side of the face at the lowest point of the lobe without curving upward
sharply demarcated, typically linear and: Sharply demarcated, typically linear and approximately horizontal, indentations in the outer surface of the ear lobe
an abnormal orientation of the earlobes: An abnormal orientation of the earlobes such that they point out- and upward. That is, the lateral surface of ear lobe faces superiorly
absence of the middle ear ossicles, mall: Absence of the middle ear ossicles, malleus, incus, and stapes
abnormality of the temporal bone of the: Abnormality of the temporal bone of the skull, which is situated at the sides and base of the skull roughly underlying the region of the face known as the temple
aplasia or developmental hypoplasia of t: Aplasia or developmental hypoplasia of the tragus
cyclopia is a congenital abnormality in: Cyclopia is a congenital abnormality in which there is only one eye. That eye is centrally placed in the area normally occupied by the root of the nose
the presence of a size difference betwee: The presence of a size difference between the left and right cornea
anisocoria, or unequal pupil size, may r: Anisocoria, or unequal pupil size, may represent a benign physiologic variant or a manifestation of disease
the presence of remnants of a fetal memb: The presence of remnants of a fetal membrane that persist as strands of tissue crossing the pupil
a malposition of the pupil owing to a de: A malposition of the pupil owing to a developmental defect of the iris
a tumor of the eye originating from cell: A tumor of the eye originating from cells of the retina
a dermal melanocytic hamartoma that pres: A dermal melanocytic hamartoma that presents as bluish hyperpigmentation on the face along the first or second branches of the trigeminal nerve. Nevus of Ota may involve the sclera
a condition associated with a limitation: A condition associated with a limitation of the horizontal ocular movement with retraction of the globe and narrowing of the palpebral fissure on adduction
persistence of the hyaloid artery, which: Persistence of the hyaloid artery, which is the embryonic artery that runs from the optic disk to the posterior lens capsule may persist; the site of attachment may form an opacity. The hyaloid artery is a branch of the ophthalmic artery, and usually regresses completely before birth
underdevelopment or absence of the nose: Underdevelopment or absence of the nose or parts thereof
abnormally increased lacrimation, that i: Abnormally increased lacrimation, that is, excessive tearing (watering eye)
complete absence of all nasal structures: Complete absence of all nasal structures
increase in bulk of the ala nasi: Increase in bulk of the ala nasi
asymmetry or size difference between the: Asymmetry or size difference between the left and right nostril
the presence of only a single nostril: The presence of only a single nostril
slender, slit-like aperture of the nostr: Slender, slit-like aperture of the nostril
the presence of more than two nostrils: The presence of more than two nostrils
absence or underdevelopment of the nasal: Absence or underdevelopment of the nasal septum
lack or loss of the soft tissues between: Lack or loss of the soft tissues between the zygomata and mandible
lack of symmetry between the left and ri: Lack of symmetry between the left and right mandible
the presence of an asymmetric mouth: The presence of an asymmetric mouth
complete or partial duplication of the p: Complete or partial duplication of the phalanges of the thumb. Depending on the severity, the appearance on x-ray can vary from a notched phalanx (the duplicated bone is almost completely fused with the phalanx), a partially fused appearance of the two bones (bifid), two separate bones appearing side to side, or completely duplicated phalanges (proximal and distal phalanx of the thumb and/or 1st metacarpal). In contrast to the phalanges of the digits 2-5 (proximal, middle and distal), the proximal phalanx of the thumb is embryologically equivalent to the middle phalanges of the other digits, whereas the first metacarpal is embryologically of phalangeal origin and as such equivalent to the proximal phalanges of the other digits
a complete duplication affecting one or: A complete duplication affecting one or more of the phalanges of the thumb. As opposed to a partial duplication were there is still a variable degree of fusion between the duplicated bones, a complete duplication leads to two separate bones appearing side to side (radio-ulnar axis) as seen on x-rays. A duplication leading to an accesory bone appearing in the proximo-distal axis on x-rays, this is actually a different entity called a Pseudoepiphyses (see according terms) sometimes also refered to as Hyperphalangism
a partial duplication, depending on seve: A partial duplication, depending on severity leading to a broad or bifid appearance, affecting one or more of the phalanges of the thumb. As opposed to a complete duplication there is still a variable degree of fusion between the duplicated bones
this term applies if one or more of the: This term applies if one or more of the phalanges of the 2nd finger are either partially duplicated, depending on severity leading to a broad or bifid appearance of the phalanges, or completely duplicated

a structural anomaly of the first metaca: A structural anomaly of the first metacarpal
any abnormality of the second metacarpal: Any abnormality of the second metacarpal bone
any abnormality of the third metacarpal: Any abnormality of the third metacarpal bone
any abnormality of the fourth metacarpal: Any abnormality of the fourth metacarpal bone

in contrast to the metacarpals 2-5, the: In contrast to the metacarpals 2-5, the first metacarpal is embryologically of phalangeal origin and as such equivalent to the proximal phalanges of the digits 2-5 (whereas the proximal phalanx of the thumb is equivalent to the middle phalanges of the other digits). The epiphysis of the first metacarpal is localized at the proximal end (as seen in the proximal phalanges of the other digits), whereas the epiphyses of the other metacarpal bones are located at the distal end. This term applies if the epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal is in any way abnormal, referring to age and gender depending norms, as seen on x-rays
an epiphysis that curves around from its: An epiphysis that curves around from its transverse orientation to a longitudinal one from proximal to distal along one side of the phalanx, thus resembling the letter 'C' and forming a bracket around the diaphysis. This results in a so called delta phalanx characterized by a triangular or trapezoidal shaped bone with a C-shaped epiphyseal plate
a cone-shaped appearance of the epiphysi: A cone-shaped appearance of the epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal, producing a 'ball-in-a-socket' appearance
abnormally large size of the epiphyses o: Abnormally large size of the epiphyses of the 1st metacarpal with respect to age-dependent norms
epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal having m: Epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal having multiple bony fragments
uneven radiographic opacity of the epiph: Uneven radiographic opacity of the epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal
the epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal are: The epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal are hard and dense like ivory. Such an epiphysis has a uniformly dense appearance on radiographs
the epiphysis of the first metacarpal is: The epiphysis of the first metacarpal is localized at the proximal end of the metacarpal bone although an accessory epiphysis may be located at the distal end of the metacarpal
abnormally small size of the epiphysis o: Abnormally small size of the epiphysis of the 1st metacarpal with respect to age-dependent norms
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in the epiphyses of the first metacarpal bone
aplasia or hypoplasia affecting the 1st: Aplasia or Hypoplasia affecting the 1st metacarpal. the presence of a supernumerary toe (not: The presence of a supernumerary toe (not a hallux) involving the third or fourth metatarsal with associated osseous syndactyly
failure to form (agenesis) of one or mor: Failure to form (agenesis) of one or more epiphyses of the big toe
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in the epiphyses of the hallux
in contrast to the metatarsals 2-5, the: In contrast to the metatarsals 2-5, the first metatarsal is embryologically of phalangeal origin and as such equivalent to the proximal phalanges of the digits 2-5, whereas the proximal phalanx of the big toe is equivalent to the middle phalanges of the other digits. This term applies to abnormalities of the epiphysis of the first metatarsal bone

In contrast to the metatarsals 2-5, the first metatarsal is embryologically of phalangeal origin and as such equivalent to the proximal phalanges of the digits 2-5, whereas the proximal phalanx of the big toe is equivalent to the middle phalanges of the other digits. This term applies to abnormalities affecting the proximal phalanx of the hallux
failure to form (agenesis) of the epiphy: Failure to form (agenesis) of the epiphysis of the proximal phalanx of the hallux
the epiphysis of the proximal phalanx of: The epiphysis of the proximal phalanx of the hallux surrounds the diaphysis, having a bracket-like form
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in the epiphyses of the proximal phalanx of the hallux
failure to form (agenesis) of the epiphy: Failure to form (agenesis) of the epiphysis of the distal phalanx of the hallux
the epiphysis of the distal phalanx of t: The epiphysis of the distal phalanx of the hallux surrounds the diaphysis, having a bracket-like form
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in the epiphyses of the distal phalanx of the hallux
failure to form (agenesis) of the epiphy: Failure to form (agenesis) of the epiphysis of the 1st metatarsal
the epiphysis of the 1st metatarsal surr: The epiphysis of the 1st metatarsal surrounds the diaphysis, having a bracket-like form
a conical (cone-shaped) appearance of th: A conical (cone-shaped) appearance of the epiphysis of the first metatarsal of the foot
the epiphysis of the 1st metatarsal are: The epiphysis of the 1st metatarsal are hard and dense like ivory. Such an epiphysis has a uniformly dense appearance on radiographs
absence of the epiphyses of the phalange: Absence of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the toes
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in the epiphyses of phalanges of the toes
increased width of phalanx of one or mor: Increased width of phalanx of one or more toes
an abnormal morphology of one or more ph: An abnormal morphology of one or more phalanges of the toes, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of one or more toe phalanges
uneven (irregular) increase in bone dens: Uneven (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the phalanges of the foot
partial/complete duplication of one or m: Partial/complete duplication of one or more phalanx of toe
a morphological anomaly of one or more p: A morphological anomaly of one or more proximal phalanges of one or more toes
absence or underdevelopment of the dista: Absence or underdevelopment of the distal phalanges of the toes
increased width of the distal phalanx of: Increased width of the distal phalanx of toe of one or more toes
an abnormal morphology of one or more di: An abnormal morphology of one or more distal phalanges of the toes, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of one or more distal toe phalanges
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the distal phalanges of the toes. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
a partial or complete duplication of one: A partial or complete duplication of one or more distal phalanx of toe
an abnormal morphology of one or more mi: An abnormal morphology of one or more middle phalanges of the toes, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of one or more middle toe phalanges
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the middle phalanges of the toes. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
partial or complete duplication of a mid: Partial or complete duplication of a middle phalanx of toe
absence (agenesis) or underdevelopment o: Absence (agenesis) or underdevelopment of one or more of the proximal phalanges of the toes
an increase in width of one ore more pro: An increase in width of one ore more proximal toe phalanges
an abnormal morphology of one or more of: An abnormal morphology of one or more of the proximal phalanges of the toes, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight sha: A deviation from the normal straight shape of a proximal phalanx of one or more toes
dissolution or degeneration of bone tiss: Dissolution or degeneration of bone tissue of the proximal toe phalanx
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the proximal phalanges of the toes. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
partial/complete duplication of a proxim: Partial/complete duplication of a proximal phalanx of toe
one or more bent (flexed) joints of the: One or more bent (flexed) joints of the first (big) toe that cannot be straightened actively or passively
chronic loss of joint motion in the tars: Chronic loss of joint motion in the tarsometatarsal joint of the hallux due to structural changes in non-bony tissue. The tarsometatarsal joints of the feet are also called Lisfranc's joints
the interphalangeal joint of the big toe: The interphalangeal joint of the big toe cannot be straightened actively or passively
the joint between the first metatarsal a: The joint between the first metatarsal and the proximal phalanx of the first (big) toe cannot be straightened actively or passively
a foot deformity resulting due to an abn: A foot deformity resulting due to an abnormality affecting the bones of the foot (as well as muscle and soft tissue). In contrast if only the muscle and soft tissue are affected the term positional foot deformity applies
any abnormality of the epiphysis of the: Any abnormality of the epiphysis of the second metacarpal bone
the normal epiphysis of the second metac: The normal epiphysis of the second metacarpal is localised at the distal end of the metacarpal bone. This term aplies if an accesory epiphysis, located at the proximal end of the metacarpal bone, is present
any abnormality of the epiphysis of the: Any abnormality of the epiphysis of the third metacarpal bone
the normal epiphysis of the third metaca: The normal epiphysis of the third metacarpal is localised at the distal end of the metacarpal bone. This term aplies if an accesory epiphysis, located at the proximal end of the metacarpal bone, is present
any abnormality of the epiphysis of the: Any abnormality of the epiphysis of the 4th metacarpal bone
the normal epiphysis of the fourth metac: The normal epiphysis of the fourth metacarpal is localised at the distal end of the metacarpal bone. This term aplies if an accesory epiphysis, located at the proximal end of the metacarpal bone, is present
any abnormality of the epiphysis of the: Any abnormality of the epiphysis of the fifth metacarpal bone
the normal epiphysis of the fifth metaca: The normal epiphysis of the fifth metacarpal is localised at the distal end of the metacarpal bone. This term aplies if an accesory epiphysis, located at the proximal end of the metacarpal bone, is present
absence of one or more epiphyses of the: Absence of one or more epiphyses of the phalanges fingers
bracket epiphysis refers to an abnormali: Bracket epiphysis refers to an abnormality in which the epiphysis surrounds a phalangeal bone, having a bracket-like form and reaching from the proximal side of a phalanx to the distal side
a cone-shaped appearance of the epiphyse: A cone-shaped appearance of the epiphyses of the fingers of the hand, producing a 'ball-in-a-socket' appearance. The related entity 'angel-shaped' epiphysis refers to a pronounced cone-shaped epiphysis in combination with a pseudoepiphysis at the distal end of a phalanx
abnormally large size of the epiphyses o: Abnormally large size of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the fingers with respect to age-dependent norms
fragmented appearance of the epiphyses o: Fragmented appearance of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the fingers
irregular radiographic opacity of the ep: Irregular radiographic opacity of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the fingers
sclerosis of the epiphyses of the phalan: Sclerosis of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the fingers, leading to an increased degree of radiopacity (white or ivory appearance) in X-rays
a secondary ossification center in the p: A secondary ossification center in the phalanges of the hand that is distinct from the normal epiphysis that does not contribute to the longitudinal growth of a tubular bone
abnormally small size of the epiphyses o: Abnormally small size of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the fingers with respect to age-dependent norms
the presence of abnormal punctate (speck: The presence of abnormal punctate (speckled, dot-like) calcifications in the epiphyses of phalanges of the fingers
a triangular appearance of the epiphyses: A triangular appearance of the epiphyses of the phalanges of the fingers of the hand
absence of one or more middle phalanx of: Absence of one or more middle phalanx of a finger
congenital hypoplasia of one or more pro: Congenital hypoplasia of one or more proximal phalanx of finger
any anomaly of distal epiphysis of phala: Any anomaly of distal epiphysis of phalanx of finger
an abnormality of the distal phalanges o: An abnormality of the distal phalanges of the hand in which the epiphysis surrounds a phalangeal bone, having a bracket-like form and reaching from the proximal side of a phalanx to the distal side
distal epiphyses of the hand are hard an: Distal epiphyses of the hand are hard and dense like ivory. Such an epiphysis has a uniformly dense appearance on radiographs
a secondary ossification center in the d: A secondary ossification center in the distal phalanges of the hand that is distinct from the normal epiphysis that does not contribute to the longitudinal growth of a tubular bone
an abnormality of the middle phalanges o: An abnormality of the middle phalanges of the hand in which the epiphysis surrounds a phalangeal bone, having a bracket-like form and reaching from the proximal side of a phalanx to the distal side
fragmented appearance of the epiphyses o: Fragmented appearance of the epiphyses of the middle phalanges of the hand
epiphyses of the middle phalanges of the: Epiphyses of the middle phalanges of the hand are hard and dense like ivory. Such an epiphysis has a uniformly dense appearance on radiographs
a secondary ossification center in the m: A secondary ossification center in the middle phalanges of the hand that is distinct from the normal epiphysis that does not contribute to the longitudinal growth of a tubular bone
an abnormality of the proximal phalanges: An abnormality of the proximal phalanges of the hand in which the epiphysis surrounds a phalangeal bone, having a bracket-like form and reaching from the proximal side of a phalanx to the distal side
epiphyses of the proximal phalanges of t: Epiphyses of the proximal phalanges of the hand are hard and dense like ivory. Such an epiphysis has a uniformly dense appearance on radiographs
a secondary ossification center in the p: A secondary ossification center in the proximal phalanges of the hand that is distinct from the normal epiphysis that does not contribute to the longitudinal growth of a tubular bone
stomatitis is an inflammation of the muc: Stomatitis is an inflammation of the mucous membranes of any of the structures in the mouth
a gap in the lower lip: A gap in the lower lip
height of the vermilion of the medial pa: Height of the vermilion of the medial part of the lower lip more than 2 SD below the mean. Alternatively, an apparently reduced height of the vermilion of the lower lip in the frontal view (subjective)
increased pigmentation, either focal or: Increased pigmentation, either focal or generalized, of the mucosa of the mouth
fibrous band between the mucosal surface: Fibrous band between the mucosal surfaces of the upper and lower alveolar ridges
any abnormality of the salivary glands,: Any abnormality of the salivary glands, the exocrine glands that produce saliva
any abnormality of the submandibular gla: Any abnormality of the submandibular glands, which are the salivary glands that are located beneath the floor of the mouth, superior to the digastric muscles
any abnormality of the sublingual glands: Any abnormality of the sublingual glands, which are the salivary glands that are located beneath the floor of the mouth anterior to the submandibular glands
a gap (cleft) affecting one of the alveo: A gap (cleft) affecting one of the alveolar ridges, which are the protuberances in the mouth that contain the sockets (alveoli) of the teeth. An alveolar cleft can affect all structures of the alveolar ridge, including the gingiva, other mucosa, periosteum, alveolar bone, and teeth
distance between the labial point of the: Distance between the labial point of the incisive papilla to the midline junction of the hard and soft palate more than 2 SD below the mean (objective) or apparently decreased length of the hard palate (subjective)
increased size and/or number of soft tis: Increased size and/or number of soft tissue folds on the palatal side of the maxillary alveolar ridge
underdevelopment or absence of the uvula: Underdevelopment or absence of the uvula
a fistula which connects the oral cavity: A fistula which connects the oral cavity and the pharyngeal area via the aspects of the soft palate
absence or underdevelopment of the tongu: Absence or underdevelopment of the tongue
short or anteriorly attached lingual fre: Short or anteriorly attached lingual frenulum, associated with limited mobility of the tongue
tongue with a median apical indentation: Tongue with a median apical indentation or fork
glossy appearance of the entire tongue s: Glossy appearance of the entire tongue surface
a heterogeneous group of congenital spin: A heterogeneous group of congenital spinal anomalies that result from defective closure of the neural tube early in fetal life
a neoplasm affecting the spinal cord: A neoplasm affecting the spinal cord
any abnormality of the spinal meninges,: Any abnormality of the spinal meninges, the system of membranes (dura mater, the arachnoid mater, and the pia mater) which envelops the spinal cord
an outpouching of the spinal meninges: An outpouching of the spinal meninges
reduced inferior to superior extent of t: Reduced inferior to superior extent of the thorax
stridor is a high pitched sound resultin: Stridor is a high pitched sound resulting from turbulent air flow in the upper airway
the congenital absence of the sternum: The congenital absence of the sternum
the sternal cleft is a rare congenital a: The sternal cleft is a rare congenital anomaly resulting from a fusion failure of the sternum
accumulation of excessive amounts of lym: Accumulation of excessive amounts of lymphatic fluid (chyle) in the pleural cavity
absence or underdevelopment of the breas: Absence or underdevelopment of the breasts
the presence of hypertrophy of the breas: The presence of hypertrophy of the breast
absence or underdevelopment of the diaph: Absence or underdevelopment of the diaphragm
ebstein's anomaly refers to an abnormall: Ebstein's anomaly refers to an abnormally placed and deformed tricuspid valve characterized by apical displacement of the septal and posterior tricuspid valve leaflets, leading to atrialization of the right ventricle with a variable degree of malformation and displacement of the anterior leaflet
absence or underdevelopment of the abdom: Absence or underdevelopment of the abdominal musculature
an anomaly of the second toe: An anomaly of the second toe
an anomaly of the third toe: An anomaly of the third toe
an anomaly of the fourth toe: An anomaly of the fourth toe
an anomaly of the little toe: An anomaly of the little toe
an anomaly of a phalanx of second toe: An anomaly of a phalanx of second toe
one or more bent (flexed) joints of the: One or more bent (flexed) joints of the second toe that cannot be straightened actively or passively

One or more bent (flexed) joints of the fourth toe that cannot be straightened actively or passively
One or more bent (flexed) joints of the fifth toe that cannot be straightened actively or passively
an abnormal morphology of one or more ph: An abnormal morphology of one or more phalanges of the second toe, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of one or more phalanges of the second toe
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the phalanges of the second toe. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
fusion of the interphalangeal joints of: Fusion of the interphalangeal joints of the 2nd toe
partial or complete duplication of a pha: Partial or complete duplication of a phalanx of second toe
an abnormal morphology of one or more ph: An abnormal morphology of one or more phalanges of the third toe, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of one or more phalanges of the third toe
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the phalanges of the third toe. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
partial or complete duplication of phala: Partial or complete duplication of phalanx of third toe
an anomaly of the proximal phalanx of th: An anomaly of the proximal phalanx of third toe
an abnormal morphology of one or more ph: An abnormal morphology of one or more phalanges of the fourth toe, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of one or more phalanges of the fourth toe
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the phalanges of the fourth toe. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
partial or complete duplication of phala: Partial or complete duplication of phalanx of fourth toe
an abnormal morphology of one or more ph: An abnormal morphology of one or more phalanges of the fifth toe, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of one or more phalanges of the fifth toe
patchy (irregular) increase in bone dens: Patchy (irregular) increase in bone density of one or more of the phalanges of the fifth toe. This can take on many forms depending on severity and distribution as can be seen on x-rays
partial or complete duplication of one o: Partial or complete duplication of one or more phalanx of little toe
absence (agenesis) or underdevelopment o: Absence (agenesis) or underdevelopment of one or more of the proximal phalanges of the 2nd toe
an abnormal morphology of the proximal p: An abnormal morphology of the proximal phalanx of the 2nd toe, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of the proximal phalanx of the 2nd toe
partial or complete duplication of proxi: Partial or complete duplication of proximal phalanx of second toe
an abnormal morphology of the middle pha: An abnormal morphology of the middle phalanx of the second toe, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of the middle phalanx of the 2nd toe
partial or complete duplication of middl: Partial or complete duplication of middle phalanx of second toe
an abnormal morphology of the distal pha: An abnormal morphology of the distal phalanx of the second toe, with a short and wide phalanx that tapers distally. Bullet-shaped phalanges lack the normal diaphyseal constriction
a deviation from the normal straight for: A deviation from the normal straight form of the distal phalanx of the 2nd toe
partial or complete duplication of the d: Partial or complete duplication of the distal phalanx of second toe
complete duplication of proximal phalanx: Complete duplication of proximal phalanx of second toe
partial duplication of proximal phalanx: Partial duplication of proximal phalanx of second toe
complete duplication of the distal phala: Complete duplication of the distal phalanx of second toe

partial duplication of middle phalanx of: Partial duplication of middle phalanx of second toe
